notes:
# Sweep stale agent/skill dispatches across the codebase to use the new `simplify` skill and `quality-reviewer` agent

## Summary

Replace stale agent dispatches across **11 files** in this repo's source tree:
- All `code-simplifier` references (any form: agent name, agent dispatch, prose) → use the new `simplify` skill (created by agents-config-b7s; name LOCKED to `simplify` in b7s's description).
- All `superpowers:code-reviewer` references → `quality-reviewer` agent (post-merger, commit 827ef3e).

Foundation work for both agents-config-lu3.4 (which retires `ralf-it` by splitting it) AND agents-config-7bk.7 (which deletes the broken `code-simplifier` agent file). Depends on agents-config-b7s (the new `simplify` skill must exist before this bead's edits make sense). After this bead lands, the codebase is consistent: every dispatch site references the live `simplify` skill or `quality-reviewer` agent.

## Background / Motivation

Surfaced during Chunk 3 design (session 5385d9fc). Two stale-name classes break dispatches today:

1. **`superpowers:code-reviewer`** has been merged into `quality-reviewer` agent (commit 827ef3e). Old name still appears in `ralf-it/SKILL.md` and plugin formula TOMLs.
2. **`code-simplifier:code-simplifier`** is a broken agent (points at a phantom skill). Replacement target is the new portable `simplify` skill (b7s).

Why a skill, not the `/simplify` slash command:
- Slash commands can only be fired by the orchestrator session — subagents cannot.
- `/simplify` lives outside this repo (sidekick/plugin injection — see agents-config-7bk.7 NOTES). Not portable to non-sidekick installs or to Codex/Gemini.
- The new `simplify` skill (b7s) lives in `src/user/.agents/skills/simplify/` and ships via `install.sh` to all three tools. Same invocation pattern works in orchestrator, subagent, and foreign-agent contexts.

This bead does NOT create the `simplify` skill (b7s does that). It only renames dispatch sites to use it.

## Files in scope (11 files, 27 stale hits)

All paths relative to repo root. Confirmed via `grep -rn "code-simplifier\|superpowers:code-reviewer"` from repo root.

| # | File | Hits | Notes |
|---|------|------|-------|
| 1 | `src/user/.agents/skills/ralf-it/SKILL.md` | 7 (lines 51, 58, 175, 179, 242, 353, 354) | Lines 51 & 58 inside DOT graph node labels — see Implementation Notes. Line 353 is the only `superpowers:code-reviewer` hit. |
| 2 | `src/user/.claude/rules/completion-gate.md` | 2 (lines 9, 10) | This file is **append-merged** by install.sh, not replaced (see Design Notes). Edit source as usual; user may need to clean up `~/.claude/rules/completion-gate.md` manually after installer runs if duplicate entries appear. |
| 3 | `src/user/.agents/skills/test-review/SKILL.md` | 2 (lines 56, 58) | Both refer to `code-simplifier` agent in dispatch tables. |
| 4 | `src/user/.agents/skills/wait-for-pr-comments/SKILL.md` | 1 (line 360) | Inline gate sequence. |
| 5 | `src/user/.agents/skills/reply-and-resolve-pr-threads/SKILL.md` | 1 (line 15) | Negative-list prose — replace `code-simplifier` with `simplify` skill (see Implementation Notes for resolved ambiguity). |
| 6 | `src/user/.agents/agents/quality-reviewer.md` | 1 (line 134) | Workflow ordering prose. |
| 7 | `src/plugins/beads/.beads/AGENTS.md` | 1 (line 113) | Plugin-tree completion-gate description. |
| 8 | `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml` | 4 (lines 188, 215, 235, 237) | Live formula dispatch sites — drives bead workflows. |
| 9 | `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml` | 4 (lines 185, 202, 204, 206) | Live formula dispatch sites. |
| 10 | `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml` | 3 (lines 66, 73, 74) | Live formula dispatch sites. |
| 11 | `README.md` | 1 (line 53) | "Shipping agents" enumeration — `code-simplifier` is in the list of OUR agents. Once 7bk.7 deletes it, this enumeration must drop the entry. |

The implementer must re-grep each file before editing — line numbers may shift slightly with prior edits.

## Carved out of scope (with rationale)

- **`README.md` line 9 and `AGENTS.md` line 13** describe what the upstream `obra/superpowers` plugin provides ("Provides the skill/agent framework … code-reviewer, code-simplifier, …"). Different concern — upstream-plugin documentation accuracy. Tracked separately in **agents-config-vie**.
- **`docs/specs/2026-04-26-pr-review-skill-redesign.md`** lines 22, 106 — historical point-in-time spec record. Editing it would be revisionism. Leave alone.

## Implementation Notes (gotchas surfaced in fresh-eyes spec review)

- **DOT graph context (file 1, lines 51 & 58)**: Inside Graphviz `label="..."` strings with literal `\n` substrings (escaped, not actual newlines). Edit as a single-line string replacement — do not treat as multi-line edits. Preserve the `\n` literals.
- **"agent" noun gotcha (multiple locations)**: Sentences currently say "code-simplifier **agent**". Replacement is "the **`simplify` skill**" — drop the noun "agent" or the result reads as "simplify skill agent". Check surrounding prose for grammar.
- **Negative-reference prose (file 5, line 15) — RESOLVED**: The line currently reads `"No \`quality-reviewer\`, no \`code-simplifier\`, no \`verify-checklist\`, no \`git commit\`, no \`git push\`."` After this bead, replace `code-simplifier` with `the simplify skill`. The skill being documented (reply-and-resolve-pr-threads) doesn't run quality-gate steps, so it shouldn't run the new simplify skill either — preserving the line's intent.
- **Workflow ordering prose (file 6, line 134)**: "before code-simplifier and verify-checklist" — replace with "before the `simplify` skill and verify-checklist". Preserve sequence semantics.
- **Formula TOML dispatch lines (files 8, 9, 10)**: These are structured `Skill:` / `Agent:` directives with specific syntax. Examples to update:
  - `Agent: superpowers:code-reviewer` → `Agent: quality-reviewer`
  - `Skill: code-simplifier:code-simplifier (run as subagent)` → `Skill: simplify` (drop the `:code-simplifier` suffix and parenthetical, or restructure per current formula conventions — implementer should read the surrounding step block)
  - `Agent: code-simplifier:code-simplifier` → `Skill: simplify` (note the directive change from `Agent:` to `Skill:`)

## Requirements

### R1. Edit only repo source-of-truth files

Edit only files under `src/`, plus the in-scope `README.md`. Do NOT edit installed copies at `~/.claude/`, `~/.codex/`, `~/.gemini/`. Per `src/user/.claude/AGENTS.md`:
- `commands/`, `skills/`, `agents/` directories: collisions are FATAL install errors, so install.sh effectively replaces (or refuses).
- `rules/*.md` files: collisions APPEND with a `---` separator. The implementer should be aware that re-running `install.sh` after editing `completion-gate.md` may leave stale paragraphs in the user's `~/.claude/rules/completion-gate.md`. This is a known install.sh quirk, not a flaw in this bead's edits — the source-of-truth file is correct.

### R2. Apply the substitutions

For each in-scope file:
- All `code-simplifier` references → use the **`simplify` skill** (literal name `simplify` is locked in agents-config-b7s).
- All `superpowers:code-reviewer` references → `quality-reviewer` agent.
- For formula TOMLs (files 8, 9, 10), the directive often changes from `Agent:` to `Skill:` — read the surrounding step block to apply the right form.

### R3. b7s must land first

Verify `src/user/.agents/skills/simplify/SKILL.md` exists before applying R2. agents-config-b7s mandates the literal name `simplify` (locked in b7s's description). If b7s lands with a different name, that's a b7s bug — escalate, do not paper over by changing this bead.

### R4. Coordination with 7bk.7 — DONE

Already completed during this bead's brainstorm session: agents-config-7bk.7 notes have been updated to reflect the architecture flip (this bead owns the dispatch rewrite; 7bk.7 narrows to deleting the broken agent file). agents-config-7bk.7 now depends on agents-config-7bk.8. **No implementer action required for R4.**

### R5. Verify with grep — strict

After all edits, run from repo root:
```
grep -rn "code-simplifier" src/ README.md
grep -rn "superpowers:code-reviewer" src/ README.md
```
Both must return empty (excluding the carved-out `AGENTS.md` line 13 — but `AGENTS.md` is not in those grep paths anyway).

If any other file under `src/` or `README.md` contains a hit not listed in the in-scope table, address it (in this bead) — the sweep must be exhaustive. Do NOT extend to `docs/`, `AGENTS.md` (root), or `~/.claude/` install copies.

### R6. Spot-check positive presence

After edits, the implementer should briefly confirm each of the 27 stale-hit locations (from the staleness table) now contains a working reference, not a silent drop. No specific count assertion — prose-context variation makes counts brittle. Report any locations that were intentionally dropped vs replaced in a brief comment on this bead.

### R7. Build/typecheck/test/preflight

This is a docs/config repo per `AGENTS.md` ("No build system, tests, or linting"). Reduces to:
- `bd preflight` clean
- Manual sanity check that formula TOMLs parse (e.g., `bd formula list` succeeds without error after edits)

## Out of Scope

- Creating the `simplify` skill itself → tracked in agents-config-b7s
- Deleting the broken `code-simplifier` agent file → tracked in agents-config-7bk.7 (now depends on this bead)
- Splitting `ralf-it` into `ralf-review` + `ralf-implement` → tracked in agents-config-lu3.4 (depends on this bead)
- Auditing upstream-plugin descriptions in `README.md` line 9 / `AGENTS.md` line 13 → tracked in agents-config-vie
- Editing historical specs under `docs/specs/`
- Editing files outside repo source (`~/.claude/`, `~/.codex/`, `~/.gemini/` install copies)
- Renaming `quality-reviewer` agent itself or modifying its body — only updating dispatch references

## Acceptance Criteria

1. `grep -rn "code-simplifier" src/ README.md` returns empty.
2. `grep -rn "superpowers:code-reviewer" src/ README.md` returns empty.
3. The 11 files in scope each contain working references to either the `simplify` skill or `quality-reviewer` agent in the locations where stale names previously appeared (spot-check; report any intentional drops in a bead comment).
4. agents-config-b7s is closed (the `simplify` skill exists at `src/user/.agents/skills/simplify/SKILL.md` per b7s's locked-name constraint).
5. `bd formula list` succeeds without parse errors after formula TOML edits.
6. `bd preflight` clean.

## Design Notes

- **Source-of-truth principle**: install.sh stages from `src/` and copies to tool homes. Editing installed copies is always wrong. Per `src/user/.claude/AGENTS.md`, install.sh has different collision semantics by directory: `skills/`/`agents/`/`commands/` collisions are fatal (replace-or-refuse); `rules/*.md` collisions append. This bead's edits are correct in source either way; install-side cleanup may be needed for the `rules/` file.
- **Skill > slash command for dispatch consistency**: A skill works in every context (orchestrator, subagent, foreign agent). A slash command works only in the orchestrator session. Standardizing on the skill form means dispatch sites can be uniform without per-site "orchestrator-tier only" guards.
- **Why this bead, not absorbed into b7s or lu3.4**: b7s creates the skill; lu3.4 splits ralf-it. Sweeping stale dispatch names is a third, distinct concern that touches files neither needs to touch. Keeping it separate keeps each bead's scope clean.
- **Why the in-scope sweep is wider than originally listed**: Cycle 1 brainstorm scoped to 1 file (ralf-it). Cycle 2 fresh-eyes review surfaced 5 additional dispatch sites + 4 plugin formula TOMLs + 1 README enumeration = 11 total. The directive ("totally consistent state") justified the expansion.
- **Why README line 9 / AGENTS.md line 13 are carved out**: They describe the upstream `obra/superpowers` plugin's offering, not our dispatch sites. Filed as agents-config-vie.
- **Why docs/specs/ is carved out**: Historical snapshots. Editing them would be revisionism. The current state, captured by today's grep, is the truth.
- **Coordination with 7bk.7**: 7bk.7 originally planned to rewrite `completion-gate.md` itself. After the architecture flip (skill, not slash command), 7bk.8 is the consistent home for that rewrite. R4 documents that 7bk.7's notes have been updated and a `bd dep add 7bk.7 7bk.8` edge has been wired — preventing out-of-order execution.

## Open Questions

None remaining.

notes:
# Spec: Remove content-destruction traps in bead formulas and skills

## Summary

Two sequential `bd update --notes=` calls in the `fix-bug` formula silently
destroy each other. Expand the scope of this bead to eliminate all content-
destruction traps across the project's beads formulas and related skills,
not just fix-bug.

## Background / motivation

Discovered while working on agents-config-smg (which was scoped to
implement-feature only).

`bd update --notes` REPLACES the bead's notes field. `bd update` has
supported `--append-notes` natively all along (appends with newline
separator). The fix-bug formula's two diagnose steps both use `--notes`,
so the second step silently destroys the first step's content.

Scott chose expanded scope (option B, session 11a30b53): audit all formulas
and skills for content-destruction traps and remove them.

### Audit results (live sites only — file-history matches ignored)

HARD TRAPS (mechanical destruction — sequential overwrites in a formula):
- `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml` line 54
  (reproduce step) and line 78 (root-cause step)

RISKY PATTERNS (single overwrite of pre-existing content; relies on agent
discipline to preserve context):
- `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` lines
  152-153 (write-spec step): `--acceptance=` and `--notes=` REPLACE any
  pre-existing content authored by create-bead or user investigation
- `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/create-bead/SKILL.md` line 99:
  `--notes=` on capture clobbers if create-bead is run twice

Good existing guards (keep as-is):
- `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml` line
  63: already warns "DO NOT call bd update --acceptance or --notes"

### Investigation options originally listed in the bead (superseded)

The original bead notes listed four fix approaches (a)-(d). All four are
workarounds for a semantic bd already supports natively via
`--append-notes`. Use the native flag wherever sequential appends are
needed.

## Requirements

### R1. fix-bug formula — replace both `--notes` calls with `--append-notes`

In `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml`:
- Line 54 (reproduce step):
  `bd update {{bead-id}} --notes "Repro: <steps>\nActual: ...\nExpected: ..."`
  →
  `bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "Repro: <steps>\nActual: ...\nExpected: ..."`
- Line 78 (root-cause step):
  `bd update {{bead-id}} --notes "Root cause: <explanation>"`
  →
  `bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "Root cause: <explanation>"`

### R2. brainstorm-bead formula — add "preserve prior context" guard to write-spec only

In `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/brainstorm-bead.formula.toml`,
add a preamble to the `write-spec` step's `description = """..."""`
triple-quoted string, positioned IMMEDIATELY ABOVE the existing
`bd update {{bead-id}} --acceptance=...` line (currently line 152).

The guard text MUST include the exact sentinel string "REPLACES the entire
notes field" so it is greppable:

  Before writing: `--notes=` REPLACES the entire notes field, and
  `--acceptance=` REPLACES the entire acceptance-criteria field. If the
  bead currently contains prior investigation context (run `bd show <id>`
  once more if in doubt — prior context typically comes from `create-bead`
  or user-added notes before brainstorming), either (a) incorporate it
  into the spec you write below, or (b) move it to a comment via
  `bd comments add` before writing.

The `revise` step (currently lines 209-210) does NOT need this guard —
by the time revise runs, the write-spec step has already replaced any
pre-existing notes with the spec document, so there is no "prior
investigation context" left to protect. Revise legitimately overwrites
the freshly-written spec with the revised spec.

Do NOT change the overwrite mechanic of either step — the write-spec
step is the canonical spec author and replacement is intentional. Only
the guard preamble is added.

### R3. create-bead skill — switch capture to `--append-notes`, add rationale for --acceptance asymmetry

In `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/create-bead/SKILL.md` line 99:
- `bd update <id> --notes="<any additional context>"`
  →
- `bd update <id> --append-notes="<any additional context>"`

Line 98's `--acceptance=` stays as-is, but add a single inline comment
line immediately above or adjacent to line 98 explaining why the
asymmetry exists, e.g.:
- `# Note: bd has no --append-acceptance; a repeat create will replace AC intentionally.`

This makes the asymmetry self-documenting for future readers who
otherwise might "fix" it inconsistently.

## Out of scope

- Changing `brainstorm-bead` write-spec or revise to use `--append-notes`.
  The write-spec step IS the canonical spec author; replacement is
  intentional. Only the guard preamble is added (R2).
- Adding append-parity flags (`--append-acceptance`, `--append-design`,
  `--append-description`) to `bd` itself — that is upstream beads-plugin
  work, not ours. File as discovered work.
- Changing `implement-feature.formula.toml` — its line 63 is a warning,
  not a trap. Leave it alone.
- Migrating brainstorm-bead's spec from the `notes` field to the
  `description` field (to match `rules/beads.md` which says "specs may be
  written directly into the bead description"). This inconsistency is
  real but orthogonal to the trap fix. File as discovered work.
- Auditing file-history / paste-cache / transcripts — those are frozen
  snapshots, not live instructions.
- Automated test coverage for formula/skill content. Verification is
  manual + grep-based (see verification block in AC).

## Acceptance criteria

- fix-bug formula's reproduce + root-cause steps use `--append-notes` (not `--notes`). Grep check: `grep -cE 'bd update .* --notes "' src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml` returns 0.
- brainstorm-bead formula's write-spec step carries the greppable guard sentinel. Grep check: `grep -c "REPLACES the entire notes field" src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` returns >=1. The sentinel MUST appear inside the write-spec step's `description = """..."""` block, positioned above the `bd update {{bead-id}} --acceptance=...` line.
- brainstorm-bead's revise step is NOT modified (no guard added there; revise legitimately overwrites).
- create-bead SKILL.md uses `--append-notes` for notes capture on what was line 99 (or wherever it migrates). Grep check: `grep -cE 'bd update <id> --notes=' src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/create-bead/SKILL.md` returns 0.
- create-bead SKILL.md contains a one-line inline comment near the `--acceptance=` invocation explaining the absence of `--append-acceptance`. Grep check: `grep -c "append-acceptance" src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/create-bead/SKILL.md` returns >=1.
- Manual verification — reproducible command block:

  ```bash
  # 1. Create throwaway bead
  THROW=$(bd create --title="throwaway: hft repro verification" --type=bug --priority=4 --json | jq -r '.id')

  # 2. Simulate reproduce step
  bd update "$THROW" --append-notes "Repro: SENTINEL-REPRO"

  # 3. Simulate root-cause step
  bd update "$THROW" --append-notes "Root cause: SENTINEL-ROOT-CAUSE"

  # 4. Confirm BOTH sentinels survive
  HITS=$(bd show "$THROW" --json | jq -r '.[0].notes' | grep -cE "SENTINEL-REPRO|SENTINEL-ROOT-CAUSE")
  [ "$HITS" = "2" ] && echo "PASS" || echo "FAIL"

  # 5. Cleanup
  bd delete "$THROW" --reason "throwaway after hft verification"
  ```

  Expected: "PASS" printed.

- No other `bd update --notes=` / `--acceptance=` / `--design=` overwrite patterns remain live in `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/` or `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/`. Grep check: `grep -rnE 'bd update [^|]*--(notes|acceptance|design)=' src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/ src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/` returns only the brainstorm-bead write-spec/revise sites and create-bead SKILL.md's `--acceptance=` line (the intentional ones), PLUS a false-positive in `implement-feature.formula.toml:63` which is warning text ("DO NOT call bd update {{bead-id}} --acceptance or --notes"), not an invocation.
- Discovered work filed (see list below).
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass. (N/A — this project has no build/typecheck/test harness for TOML/MD content; verification is manual + grep-based per above.)

## Design notes

- `bd update --append-notes` already exists and adds a newline separator —
  no shell gymnastics needed.
- `--acceptance`, `--description`, `--design` have NO `--append-*` parity
  today. Any fix for those would require upstream `bd` changes or shell
  read-modify-write wrappers. Out of scope.
- The brainstorm-bead `write-spec` guard is advisory (agent-discipline
  based), not mechanical. A mechanical fix would require restructuring
  the formula's spec-authoring step — out of scope.
- The brainstorm-bead `revise` step intentionally receives NO guard: by
  the time revise runs, write-spec has already replaced any pre-existing
  notes. The revise overwrite is by design and protects nothing beyond
  the freshly-written spec.
- `implement-feature.formula.toml:63` contains the strings `--acceptance`
  and `--notes` inside a WARNING sentence. Any future automated audit
  should match only `^\s*bd update` invocations, not the bare flag
  substrings, to avoid false-positives here. Non-blocking for this bead.

## Open questions

None remaining. Scope and approach confirmed by Scott in session 11a30b53
(2026-04-20).

## Implementation notes (CRITICAL — for the implementer)

⚠️ **META-BOOTSTRAP: DO NOT pour the `fix-bug` formula on this bead.**

This bead fixes a bug in the `fix-bug` formula itself. The broken behavior
is exactly the two `bd update --notes=` calls in reproduce + root-cause.
Pouring `fix-bug` on `agents-config-hft` would run those two steps against
this very bead and DESTROY the spec you are reading right now.

Use `start-bead` Route B (trivial / inline) with explicit subagent
dispatch for the completion gate (code-reviewer → code-simplifier →
verify-checklist) instead of pouring a formula. See the
`meta-bead-formula-bootstrapping` memory for precedent (originally
discovered on `agents-config-smg`).

`run-queue` should skip `agents-config-hft` and let a human drive
implementation via the inline route. If `run-queue` cannot be told to
skip, the operator should claim the bead manually and implement via
Route B before `run-queue` gets to it.

## Discovered work to file after implementation

1. Upstream `bd` should support `--append-acceptance`, `--append-design`,
   `--append-description` to give full append-parity with `--append-notes`.
   Non-blocking; nice-to-have.
2. `brainstorm-bead` formula writes the spec to the bead's `notes` field,
   but `rules/beads.md` says "specs may be written directly into the bead
   description". Resolve by either (a) migrating brainstorm-bead's
   write-spec step to use `--description` (via `--body-file`) and updating
   the finalize/downstream steps, or (b) updating `rules/beads.md` to
   reflect the actual `notes`-field convention.

notes:
# Spec — replace redundant `brainstorm` step in `implement-feature.formula.toml` (rev 2)

## Summary
Replace the `brainstorm` step (lines 43-66) in `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml` with a new `confirm` step that validates the bead is brainstormed, restates the plan, and never invokes `superpowers:brainstorming`. The `confirm` step has explicit branches for autonomous-mode (run-queue) vs interactive-mode (start-bead / direct human pour). No changes to `fix-bug.formula.toml`.

## Background / motivation
The pipeline is `brainstorm-bead` → produces AC + labels (`brainstormed`, `implementation-ready`) → `run-queue` pours `implement-feature` → FIRST step of the poured molecule is ANOTHER `superpowers:brainstorming` invocation. This is redundant with brainstorm-bead's work and violates the `rules/beads.md` Skill Partnership rule ("the bead description IS the plan"). It can also overwrite AC that brainstorm-bead already captured.

Evidence:
- `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml` step `brainstorm` (line 44) invokes `superpowers:brainstorming` unconditionally.
- Runtime confirmation: `mol-uaq.children` included `"Brainstorm: requirements for Split ralf-it into ralf-review + ralf-implement…"` despite `lu3.4` carrying `brainstormed` + `implementation-ready` labels.

## Scope correction (from original bead)
Original bead alleged `fix-bug.formula.toml` had the same flaw. Assessment showed `fix-bug`'s first steps are `reproduce` + `root-cause` invoking `superpowers:systematic-debugging` / `superpowers:root-cause-tracing` — bug-specific diagnosis, NOT generic brainstorming. They are not redundant in the same way. `fix-bug.formula.toml` is **out of scope** for this bead. (A SEPARATE concern in fix-bug — the `--notes` overwrite between reproduce and root-cause steps — is captured in `agents-config-hft`.)

## Requirements

### R1. Step replacement
- Remove the `brainstorm` step from `implement-feature.formula.toml`.
- Add a `confirm` step as the first step (no `needs` field).
- Update the `worktree` step's `needs = ["brainstorm"]` to `needs = ["confirm"]`.
- Update the formula's header comment (lines 11-17 phase list) and the `description` field (line 25) to reflect `confirm` instead of `brainstorm`. Update the `# Phase 1: Intent` banner to match the step's new role (e.g. "Phase 1: Intent — confirm bead is brainstormed and restate plan").

### R2. `confirm` step contract — happy path
When `bd label list {{bead-id}}` shows BOTH `brainstormed` AND `implementation-ready`:
1. `bd show {{bead-id}}` — read description and AC.
2. Restate the implementation plan in 1-3 sentences.
3. Mark the step done. The next step (worktree) runs.
4. Never invoke `superpowers:brainstorming`.

### R3. `confirm` step contract — missing-label path (TWO mechanisms)
When EITHER label is missing, the response depends on execution mode:

- **Autonomous mode** (run-queue, or any context with no human at the keyboard):
  - Escalate the bead: `bd human {{bead-id}}` with a clear note explaining the missing labels and recommending `start-bead` / `brainstorm-bead`.
  - Do NOT prompt — there is no operator to answer.
  - Abort the molecule: leave the `confirm` step open / not closed, so the molecule does not advance.
  
- **Interactive mode** (start-bead routing, or a human running `bd mol pour` directly):
  - Print a clear stdout/chat warning describing the missing labels.
  - Ask the user via natural conversation:
    - (a) Abort and route the bead through `start-bead` / brainstorm-bead first, OR
    - (b) Proceed with implementation using whatever is in the bead description.
  - On (a): mark step still-open / abort molecule.
  - On (b): proceed to worktree.

Abort mechanism (both paths): leave the `confirm` step `open` (do not close it). Print a one-line follow-up: `Molecule paused at confirm. Run brainstorm-bead on this bead and re-attempt, or 'bd mol burn <mol-id>' to discard.` Do NOT call `bd mol burn` from within the step — leave the disposition to the operator (or to run-queue policy).

Mode detection: the agent driving the molecule infers mode from its own context (was it dispatched by `run-queue`? Is it in interactive conversation with the user?). The formula step text instructs the agent to make this judgment explicitly. No formula-level interactive primitive is required — the formula step is text guidance for the agent.

### R4. No state mutation before confirm proceeds
`confirm` MUST run before any state mutation in the molecule. Specifically, the parent-chain claim walk (currently in `worktree` step around lines 87-94) is downstream of `confirm`, so this is satisfied by step ordering — no additional code needed, but the AC verifies it.

### R5. Belt-and-suspenders
The `implement-bead` skill's existing preflight label-check (`SKILL.md` L19-46) is **kept unchanged**. It guards the `implement-bead` invocation path. The new formula `confirm` step guards a DIFFERENT, narrower leak path: a human (or script) running `bd mol pour implement-feature --var bead-id=<id>` DIRECTLY, bypassing `implement-bead`. Both gates retained because they cover different paths.

## Out of scope
- `fix-bug.formula.toml` — no changes.
- Making `brainstorm-bead` type-aware for bug beads (potential follow-up).
- The `--notes` overwrite concern in fix-bug — tracked separately as `agents-config-hft`.
- Any formula-level interactive prompt primitive — explicitly chose to use agent-driven mode detection instead.

## Acceptance Criteria

**Structural (file-level):**
- AC1: `implement-feature.formula.toml` no longer contains a `[[steps]]` block with `id = "brainstorm"`.
- AC2: `implement-feature.formula.toml` contains a `[[steps]]` block with `id = "confirm"` as the first `[[steps]]` block; this block has no `needs` field.
- AC3: The `[[steps]]` block with `id = "worktree"` in `implement-feature.formula.toml` has `needs = ["confirm"]`.
- AC4: The formula's `description` field (line 25) and Phase 1 header comment are updated to reflect `confirm` (no remaining mention of "brainstorm" as the first step in headers / comments).

**Behavioral (runtime contract — described in the `confirm` step text):**
- AC5: Step text explicitly forbids invoking `superpowers:brainstorming`.
- AC6: Step text describes the happy-path (both labels present): read bead, restate plan in 1-3 sentences, proceed.
- AC7: Step text describes autonomous-mode missing-label path: call `bd human {{bead-id}}`, do not prompt, leave step open / abort molecule.
- AC8: Step text describes interactive-mode missing-label path: print warning, ask user (a) abort or (b) proceed; honor the choice.
- AC9: Step text instructs the agent to detect autonomous vs interactive mode from its own context (run-queue dispatch ⇒ autonomous; user-present ⇒ interactive).

**Negative space:**
- AC10: `fix-bug.formula.toml` has no semantic changes (incidental whitespace permitted only if introduced by tooling).
- AC11: `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md` label preflight (lines 19-46 area) is functionally unchanged AND no SKILL.md prose elsewhere references the removed `brainstorm` step (cross-check during implementation).
- AC12: `confirm` step description includes the exact abort-mechanism guidance (leave step `open`; print operator follow-up; do not call `bd mol burn` from within the step).

## Design notes
- **Two-path missing-label handling** addresses C1 from the adversarial review: a single "interactively ask" mechanism would deadlock run-queue. Splitting by mode keeps autonomous flow safe (escalate via `bd human`) while preserving the interactive escape hatch the user requested.
- **Behavioral AC alongside structural AC** addresses C2: structural AC alone would let a future implementer pass the spec by inserting the right strings into the description without honoring the contract at runtime. Step text describing each branch is the closest the formula gets to a runtime check (the agent driving the step is the executor).
- **Belt-and-suspenders justified by leak path** addresses N3: the skill preflight covers `implement-bead` invocations. The formula confirm covers direct `bd mol pour` invocations (operator console use, scripts, imports). Two distinct entry points, two gates.
- **Docs drift covered by AC4** addresses N2.
- **Mode detection via agent judgment** is the simplest design — no new formula primitive. Risk: an agent misreading the mode. Mitigation: the step text gives explicit cues ("dispatched by run-queue ⇒ autonomous").
- **start-bead Route B / no conflict** (M4): start-bead routes a NOT-ready bead to brainstorm-bead BEFORE implement-feature is ever poured. The new confirm step lives INSIDE implement-feature, which runs only AFTER routing. No overlap.

## Open questions
- C1 (mechanism for missing-label response) — **resolved** by R3 / two-path handling.
- C2 (behavioral vs grep-bait AC) — **resolved** by adding behavioral AC5–AC9.
- n3 (abort mechanism) — **resolved** by R3 abort-mechanism paragraph and AC12.
- None remaining.

---

## Original bead context (preserved)
Context: the bead pipeline is brainstorm-bead → produces AC + labels (brainstormed, implementation-ready) → run-queue pours implement-feature → FIRST step of the poured molecule is ANOTHER brainstorm. The second brainstorm is redundant with brainstorm-bead's work and violates the beads.md Skill Partnership rule ('the bead description IS the plan').

Evidence:
- src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml Phase 1 Intent step 'brainstorm' (lines 43-66 approx) unconditionally invokes superpowers:brainstorming.
- Confirmed at runtime: mol-uaq.children includes 'Brainstorm: requirements for Split ralf-it into ralf-review + ralf-implement…' despite lu3.4 carrying the brainstormed + implementation-ready labels.
- fix-bug.formula.toml was originally claimed to have the same flaw — reassessment showed this is false; fix-bug has bug-specific diagnosis steps, not redundant generic brainstorming. (Separate fix-bug concern about --notes overwriting tracked in agents-config-hft.)

Impact: double-brainstorming wastes cycles, can produce conflicting AC (the second brainstorm may overwrite the first), and undermines the whole brainstorm-bead → run-queue pipeline.
close_reason: Replaced redundant brainstorm step with confirm step in implement-feature.formula.toml. PR #16 merged (squash). Two-path handling (autonomous bd human + abort vs interactive warn+ask). Three Copilot mechanical fixes applied inline (string wrapping).

title: bd mol pour does not set parent on created molecule to target bead
notes:
Context: after run-queue poured implement-feature on lu3.4, the resulting molecule mol-uaq has parent=None, not lu3.4. Same issue on mol-gs8 (prior pour for Divergence 4 bead). No structural link exists from a molecule back to the bead it was poured for.

Repro:
  bd mol pour implement-feature --var bead-id=<bead-id> --var feature='<title>'
  bd show <new-mol-id> --json | python3 -c 'import sys,json; r=json.load(sys.stdin); r=r[0] if isinstance(r,list) else r; print(r.get("parent"))'
  # → None

Expected: new molecule's parent field points at the target bead, OR some other structural edge (dependency type, metadata field) encodes the 'molecule-for-bead' relationship so that 'find molecules for bead X' is answerable via bd list.

Impact: the 'does a molecule already exist for this bead?' check in start-bead Step 2 and run-queue orchestration is STRUCTURALLY impossible. The check always returns empty even when a live molecule exists. Combined with the text-output bug (filed separately), run-queue will re-pour molecules instead of resuming existing ones.

Possible fixes: (a) set parent = <bead-id> when pouring with --var bead-id=<id>; (b) add a 'molecule-for-bead' metadata/edge; (c) encode as a dependency.
close_reason: Worked around consumer-side in PR #15: stamp for-bead-<id> label on molecules at pour/wisp; probe with bd list --label ... --type molecule --json. Upstream beads bugs remain.

title: bd list text output ignores --parent and --type filters (JSON correct)
notes:
Context: discovered while run-queue checked for existing molecules on lu3.4.

Repro (run from this project):
  bd list --parent agents-config-lu3.4                       # returns lu3.4 ITSELF in text
  bd list --parent agents-config-lu3.4 --type mol            # returns lu3.4 (type=task) in text — both filters ignored
  bd list --parent agents-config-lu3.4 --json                # returns []
  bd list --parent agents-config-lu3.4 --type mol --json     # returns []

Actual: text output ignores --parent and --type filters; displays the referenced bead (or possibly the session's last-touched issue).
Expected: text output applies filters identically to JSON output.

Impact: any agent/skill/script relying on the text output is misled. In particular:
- start-bead Step 2 ('Check for existing molecule') uses 'bd list --parent <bead-id> --type mol 2>/dev/null' in text mode.
- run-queue uses the same pattern.
Both therefore ALWAYS find the bead itself in text and conclude 'a molecule exists' OR ignore that result — either way the check is unreliable.

Workaround until fixed: consumers must use --json and parse accordingly.
close_reason: Worked around consumer-side in PR #15: stamp for-bead-<id> label on molecules at pour/wisp; probe with bd list --label ... --type molecule --json. Upstream beads bugs remain.

title: Divergence 1: wisp formulas not materializing step beads (start-bead + formulas)
description:
# Root cause

`wisp.go:249-251` in the upstream `bd` binary forces root-only materialization unless the formula declares `pour = true`:

```go
if !rootOnly && subgraph != nil && !subgraph.Pour {
    rootOnly = true
}
```

The `brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` has `phase = "vapor"` but no `pour = true`. When wisped, only a root issue is created — no child step beads are materialized. `bd mol current` returns "0/0 steps", the agent has no step IDs to operate on, and the CLI's follow-up output (`bd close <root>.<step>`) misleadingly suggests steps exist.

The `pour` field is misleadingly named: it means "materialize children," not "use pour command." Formulas need `pour = true` for `bd mol wisp` to produce actionable step beads. `bd mol pour` materializes children regardless.

Secondary cause: `start-bead` Route C says "wisp the formula, then execute as MAIN AGENT" with no execution loop. Even with a materialized molecule, the agent has no contract for how to drive it.

# Edits required

## Formulas (src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/)

Add `pour = true` to all four formulas:

- `brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` (vapor — only ever wisped, bug fires)
- `merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml` (vapor — only ever wisped, bug fires)
- `implement-feature.formula.toml` (liquid — defensive)
- `fix-bug.formula.toml` (liquid — defensive)

Update `# Usage:` comments in `brainstorm-bead` and `merge-and-cleanup` to add next-step navigation:

    # bd mol current <wisp-id>   # shows steps with status

## Skill (src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/start-bead/SKILL.md)

Replace Route C action block with an explicit execution loop:

    Action: wisp the brainstorm-bead formula:
      bd mol wisp create brainstorm-bead --var bead-id=<id>

    Then drive the molecule as the MAIN AGENT (brainstorming requires
    interactive conversation — do NOT dispatch a subagent):

      1. bd mol current <wisp-id>           # shows all steps with markers
      2. For the first [ready] step:
         - bd update <step-id> --claim       # lock as in_progress
         - bd show <step-id>                  # read step instructions
         - Execute the step (interactive for discuss)
         - bd close <step-id> --continue      # closes + auto-claims next
      3. Repeat until all steps are [done]

    Use step IDs from `bd mol current` output — do NOT synthesize
    `<root>.<step>` patterns. Step IDs are independent hash-based.

Add a failure-mode block under Route C:

    ### Failure mode: 0/0 steps after wisp creation

    If `bd mol current <wisp-id>` shows "0/0 steps complete" with no
    step list, the formula is missing `pour = true`.
      bd mol burn <wisp-id>
    Tell the user it is a formula bug. Do NOT fall back to invoking
    the brainstorming skill inline.

    For other mid-run abandonment (partial progress made), prefer
      bd mol squash <wisp-id> --summary "Aborted: <reason>"
    to preserve history for debugging.

Add two Red Flags rows:

    | "The molecule looks empty, I'll just do the work inline" | STOP. 0/0 = formula bug. Burn + report. |
    | "I'll make up step IDs — they look like <root>.<step>"  | No. Use IDs from bd mol current output. |

# Design decisions

- **Defensive `pour = true` on all four formulas** (not just vapor ones): zero downside, protects against future wisp misuse of liquid formulas.
- **`bd close --continue` in the loop**: cleanest auto-advance; saves one `bd mol current` query per step.
- **Burn vs squash recovery**: burn only for the 0/0 formula-bug case (nothing to preserve); squash for any partial-progress abandonment.
- **No new "execute-molecule" skill**: 5-line loop fits inline in Route C. `implement-bead` has a different pattern (dispatches subagents between steps) so cannot reuse the same verbatim loop.
- **Keep `bd mol wisp create`** as the command in Route C text: `create` is a valid subcommand (back-compat per `wisp.go:813`). Changing to bare `bd mol wisp` is churn with no behavior delta.

# Acceptance criteria

- All four formulas declare `pour = true`
- `brainstorm-bead` and `merge-and-cleanup` formula header comments include `bd mol current <wisp-id>` guidance
- `start-bead` Route C has explicit execution loop with `bd update --claim` / `bd show` / execute / `bd close --continue`
- `start-bead` has a failure-mode block for the 0/0-steps case with burn-vs-squash guidance
- `start-bead` Red Flags table includes the two new entries
- Manual verification: wisp `brainstorm-bead` against a test bead, confirm child step beads are materialized, confirm `bd mol current` surfaces step IDs
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

# Notes

- Discovered during audit of discord-agent-scaffold session `64012e00`
- Related upstream `bd` CLI bug (misleading `bd close <root>.<step>` output at `wisp.go:303` when wisp is root-only) — tracked separately in the beads project issue tracker
- Expert consultation confirmed root cause via `wisp.go:249-251`, `pour.go:216`, and `mol_current.go:191-238` source evidence
close_reason: pour=true on all 4 formulas + start-bead Route C execution loop + failure mode + Red Flags

title: Harden bead-driven workflow boundaries (audit fixes + follow-on strategy)
description:
# Problem statement

AI agents working bead-tracked tasks silently bypass the formula/molecule protocol because of three compounding issues identified in the audit of `discord-agent-scaffold` session `64012e00`:

1. **Broken formula mechanism** — wisps were root-only by default; step beads never materialized; agents saw "0/0 steps" and abandoned the molecule.
2. **Skill-paradigm conflict** — beads (lifecycle) and superpowers (methodology) overlap on planning/execution. External superpowers skills pulled agents off the bead path via their auto-match triggers.
3. **Asymmetric authorization language** — rules emphasize "merge requires authorization" without parallel "PR creation is AUTOMATIC" language; agents over-generalize caution and stop mid-workflow.

Ralf-it also over-specifies outer-workflow concerns (worktree, delivery) that conflict with bead formulas, which emerged as a separate architectural question worth re-evaluating.

Strategy questions: after overlay rules ship, do residual risks warrant further closers? Is overlay-only sufficient or is a superpowers fork eventually needed?

# Context

- **Audit target**: discord-agent-scaffold session `64012e00-6f05-409f-95b2-243ae02e1f64`
- **Analysis session**: `c06c7b55` (April 2026)
- **Expert confirmation**: beads expert traced root causes to `wisp.go:249-251`, `pour.go:216`, `mol_current.go:191-238`
- **Upstream dep**: no changes needed in upstream `bd` binary for Phase 1; CLI output bug at `wisp.go:303` filed separately in the beads project issue tracker

# Initiative phases

## Phase 1 — Fix audit findings (P0/P1)

- **`lu3.1`** (P0) — Divergence 1: wisp formulas not materializing step beads. Add `pour = true` to four formulas; add execution loop to `start-bead` Route C; add failure-mode block for 0/0 state.
- **`lu3.2`** (P1) — Divergence 2: beads ↔ superpowers partnership contract. Rule section listing inner tools vs off-limits skills; formula step citations of methodology skills; red flags in start-bead and implement-bead. Scope also includes `implement-feature` delivery step citation (moved from lu3.3).
- **`lu3.3`** (P1) — Divergence 3: clarify delivery authorization boundaries. Action categorization (AUTOMATIC vs AUTHORIZATION-REQUIRED); "ready when you are" red flag; sharpen completion-gate HARD STOP; new beads overlay file for bead-aware delivery guidance.
- **`lu3.4`** (P1) — Re-evaluate ralf-it form (skill vs command vs agent). Design bead — needs brainstorming to decide form. Emerged from Divergence 3 analysis as a cleaner fix than patching ralf-it's over-specified workflow declarations in place.

## Phase 1.5 — Evaluate risk-closers (P3)

- **`717`** (P3) — After Phase 1 ships and is observed for drift rate, brainstorm and decide whether to implement: hook-level enforcement, user-direction guardrail, session-audit cadence, broader failure-mode block. Each option gets a `bd decision` record. Blocks Phase 2.

## Phase 2 — Fork superpowers if needed (P3)

- **`cx6`** (P3) — If overlay rules + risk-closers still leave drift, fork superpowers into a curated bead-aware plugin. Eliminates the three competing skills entirely; keeps the methodology keepers. Candidate names: `superbeadpowers`, `superbeads`, `beadpowers`. Blocked by `717` — cannot proceed until risk-closer evaluation completes.

# Scope

**In scope:**
- Formula TOML and skill text changes in `src/plugins/beads/`
- Core rule updates in `src/user/.claude/rules/`
- New beads overlay files in `src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/` (appends via install mechanism)
- Ralf-it form re-evaluation and follow-on restructuring
- Strategic evaluation of risk-closers and fork option

**Out of scope:**
- Upstream `bd` CLI bugs (tracked in the beads project issue tracker)
- Non-bead workflow changes (superpowers usage outside bead context)
- Changes to the external superpowers plugin itself (unless Phase 2 fork is authorized)

# Acceptance criteria

The epic is complete when ALL of the following hold:

1. **Phase 1 closed**: `lu3.1`, `lu3.2`, `lu3.3`, `lu3.4` are all closed with their own acceptance criteria met.
2. **Observation**: the partnership contract has been observed in at least 2 subsequent session audits; drift rate documented.
3. **Phase 1.5 resolved**: `717` is closed — each risk-closer option has a `bd decision` (accepted/deferred/rejected with rationale). Accepted options are implemented as sub-beads.
4. **Phase 2 resolved**: `cx6` is either closed as "implemented" OR closed as "not needed — overlay + risk-closers sufficient" with rationale.
5. **End-to-end behavior**: an agent running `start-bead` on a fresh feature bead can execute the full `brainstorm → implement → deliver` pipeline without silently falling back to direct skill invocations or manual user handoffs.
6. **Quality gate**: Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

# Notes

- Phase numbering mirrors the time horizon: Phase 1 is immediate, Phase 1.5 is post-observation, Phase 2 is conditional escalation.
- The epic description was rewritten in session `c06c7b55` to cover all three phases under a single umbrella (was previously scoped to audit fixes only).

title: install.sh: --prune 'all' selection clobbers PATH via zsh `path` tied-array, breaks backup()
notes:
Reproduction: ./scripts/install.sh --prune-only, then 'a' at the prompt under zsh on macOS.

Symptom: 'backup:5: command not found: date' (and presumably also 'rm', but error short-circuits at date first).

Root cause: zsh has a special variable `path` (lowercase) that is a tied array to `PATH` (uppercase). Assigning to local `path` reassigns `PATH` to the orphan path string for the duration of the calling function. _delete_orphan does `local tool=\"$1\" path=\"$2\"`, calls backup() which calls `date` — `date` not found because PATH is now `/Users/scott/.claude/skills/resolve-pr-comments` (the first orphan).

Affected lines in scripts/install.sh:
- 1084: _display_orphans local declaration includes 'path' (cosmetically wrong, may not crash since loop body uses only printf builtin)
- 1109: _delete_orphan local — THE BUG that breaks 'a' selection
- 1183-1189: per-item interactive loop ('o' selection) — same bug; would crash on the first y/Y selection too

Fix: rename 'path' to a safe name in all three sites. 'orphan_path' is clearest; 'p' is shortest. Affects local declarations and references inside those functions.

Verification: under expect with PTY, run `./scripts/install.sh --prune-only` then send 'a' — should complete without 'command not found' errors. Same with 'o' followed by 'y'.

Severity: P1 — feature is broken on macOS where zsh re-exec is mandatory (system /bin/sh is bash 3.2, so the script ALWAYS re-execs to zsh on macOS).
close_reason: Fixed in PR #27 (squash-merged as 53d7ed2). Renamed local 'path' to 'orphan_path' in three sites in scripts/install.sh: _delete_orphan, _display_orphans, and per-item interactive loop. Added warning comments at all three sites referencing the zsh tied-array gotcha.

title: Create simplify skill (port from /simplify command body)
description:
Create a portable `simplify` skill at `src/user/.agents/skills/simplify/SKILL.md`, ported from the existing `/simplify` slash command body. **The skill name MUST be exactly `simplify`** — this is a hard constraint, not a brainstorm choice. Downstream sweep bead agents-config-7bk.8 literal-references the name `simplify` in 25+ dispatch sites; renaming would break them.

Goal: a single canonical invocation site ("use the simplify skill") that works for orchestrator, subagent, and foreign-agent contexts — eliminating the orchestrator-tier-only guard required by slash-command-based dispatch.

## Why a skill instead of fixing the slash command in place

- Slash commands can only be invoked by the orchestrator (the user-driven session). Subagents cannot fire them.
- `/simplify` lives outside this repo (sidekick/plugin injection — see agents-config-7bk.7 NOTES). Not portable to non-sidekick installs or Codex/Gemini.
- A skill living in `src/user/.agents/skills/` ships via `install.sh` to all three tools.
- Skill body can detect tool capability: parallel Agent dispatch when available, serial in-context execution as fallback.

## Sketch of intended scope (refine during brainstorm)

- Port the body of `/simplify` into `src/user/.agents/skills/simplify/SKILL.md` (name LOCKED — see top of this description)
- Modify Phase 2 to do tool-capability detection — "if Agent tool available, dispatch the three reviewers (reuse, quality, efficiency) concurrently; otherwise execute the three reviews in series in the current context"
- Decide single-source-of-truth strategy for /simplify command vs simplify skill (drift mitigation): comment-header sync directive, OR rewrite slash command body to invoke the skill, OR accept periodic re-sync as known cost
- Preserve everything currently encoded in the broken `code-simplifier` agent body that's worth keeping (worktree scoping, behavior-preserving discipline, project-memory persistence, boundary rules) — already inventoried in agents-config-7bk.7 NOTES

## Coordination

- Blocks: agents-config-7bk.8 (which sweeps stale dispatches across the codebase to use the new skill name)
- Adjacent: agents-config-7bk.7 (deletes the broken `code-simplifier` agent file; will adopt the skill form for completion-gate)
- 7bk.7 should re-evaluate its current decision (orchestrator-fires-/simplify) once this skill exists — likely flip to invoking the skill instead

## Out of scope for this bead (defer to consumers)

- Renaming dispatch sites in ralf-it, completion-gate, test-review, wait-for-pr-comments, reply-and-resolve-pr-threads, quality-reviewer, plugin formula TOMLs — that's 7bk.8's job
- Deleting the broken `code-simplifier` agent file — that's 7bk.7's job
acceptance_criteria:
1. src/user/.agents/skills/simplify/SKILL.md exists with `name: simplify` frontmatter and a three-phase body (identify changes → three-axis review → aggregate and fix), retaining the extracted Phase-2 reviewer checklist verbatim.
2. The skill body adds a new `Operating Discipline` section (placed between the intro and Phase 1, or equivalent placement) that integrates the four R4 disciplines into the existing extracted body. Implementer must NOT declare the body "already done" without adding this section.
3. Phase 2 explicitly handles both runtime shapes using capability-shaped wording (NOT tool-named): "If your runtime supports concurrent subagent dispatch in a single turn (Claude `Agent`, Codex `Task`, Gemini equivalents), dispatch the three reviewers in parallel; otherwise execute the three reviews serially in this context."
4. The Operating Discipline section preserves the apply-categories list (renames, dead-code removal, trivially-redundant-branch collapse, clearly-duplicated helper extraction) and flag-categories list (changes touching return values, side effects, error paths, exported APIs, or runtime ordering) — verbatim or with equivalent enumeration. Also includes worktree scoping, "no abstractions without ≥2 call sites", boundary rules (no scope creep, no test rewrites, no new deps, no commits/pushes), and host-agnostic memory guidance.
5. Memory guidance has an explicit no-memory fallback: "If the host has no persistent memory mechanism, surface the recurring pattern in the final summary under a `Recurring pattern observed:` line so the user can record it manually."
6. The skill file's first lines contain an HTML comment header recording: source = `/simplify` slash command (sidekick injection); drift policy = (C) accept periodic re-sync; last sync = 2026-05-02. Comment is placed BEFORE the `---` frontmatter so it does not corrupt YAML parsing, OR inside the markdown body above the H1, whichever the host parsers tolerate (verify by inspection of one existing skill).
7. `scripts/install.sh --dry-run --tools=claude,codex,gemini --plugins=beads` shows the skill stages into all three CLI destinations with no fatal collision.
8. No files outside `src/user/.agents/skills/simplify/` are modified by this bead (7bk.7/7bk.8 own the rest).
9. Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
notes:
# Spec — Create simplify skill (port from /simplify command body)

## Summary

Create a portable `simplify` skill at `src/user/.agents/skills/simplify/SKILL.md` ported from the existing sidekick-injected `/simplify` slash command. The skill is invocable from orchestrator, subagent, and foreign-agent contexts (Claude/Codex/Gemini) — eliminating the orchestrator-tier-only restriction that breaks the broken `code-simplifier` agent. Skill name MUST be exactly `simplify` (hard constraint — 27 downstream dispatch sites in 7bk.8 literal-reference the name).

A prior extraction left the slash command body at `~/.claude/skills/simplify/SKILL.md`; that file has been moved (during this brainstorm session) to `src/user/.agents/skills/simplify/SKILL.md`. Implementation REFINES that body — the extracted body is missing the R4 disciplines and they must be ADDED, not assumed-present.

## Background / Motivation

- **Slash commands are orchestrator-only.** Subagents cannot fire them, breaking dispatch from `ralf-it`, `completion-gate`, plugin formula TOMLs, and other skills.
- **`/simplify` lives outside this repo** (sidekick injection — see 7bk.7 NOTES). Not portable to non-sidekick installs or to Codex/Gemini.
- **The `code-simplifier` agent is broken.** It instructs itself to invoke a `simplify` skill that did not exist. Phantom dependency.
- **A skill at `src/user/.agents/skills/simplify/`** ships via `install.sh` to all three CLIs and works in any context.
- **Foundation work for the 7bk.8 sweep**, which renames 27 dispatch sites across 11 files to use this skill.

## Requirements

### R1 — Skill file exists with locked name
`src/user/.agents/skills/simplify/SKILL.md` exists with frontmatter:
- `name: simplify` (LOCKED — install.sh stages by directory name; runtime hosts read the frontmatter `name`. Both must be `simplify`.)
- `description: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.`

Schema follows existing skills (test-review, ralf-it as templates).

### R2 — Three-phase body, extracted reviewer checklist preserved verbatim
- **Phase 1 — Identify changes**: `git diff` (or `git diff HEAD`); fall back to recently-modified files when no git changes.
- **Phase 2 — Review on three axes**: Reuse, Quality, Efficiency. Reviewer checklist preserved VERBATIM from the extracted body (`src/user/.agents/skills/simplify/SKILL.md` lines 18–50).
- **Phase 3 — Aggregate + fix**: Apply behavior-preserving fixes; flag risky items with reason; do not argue with findings.

### R3 — Capability-shaped Phase-2 dispatch
Phase 2 instructs the executing agent in CAPABILITY terms, not tool names:

> "If your runtime supports concurrent subagent dispatch in a single turn (Claude `Agent`, Codex `Task`, Gemini equivalents), dispatch the three reviewers in parallel. Otherwise, execute the three reviews serially in this context."

Both paths produce equivalent findings (caveat: serial execution can carry context bias across axes — accepted, not addressed).

### R4 — Operating Discipline section (NEW — must be ADDED to extracted body)
The extracted body is MISSING all R4 disciplines. Implementation MUST add a new `## Operating Discipline` section (placed between the intro and Phase 1, or equivalent) containing:

- **Worktree scoping** — when the caller supplies a worktree path, operate there (not the main tree). When unspecified, operate in the current working directory.
- **Apply-vs-flag classification** with explicit category lists:
  - **Apply**: renames, dead-code removal, trivially-redundant-branch collapse, clearly-duplicated helper extraction.
  - **Flag**: anything that could change a return value, a side effect, an error path, an exported API, or runtime ordering.
- **No abstractions without ≥2 call sites** — new helpers, type aliases, wrappers require concrete evidence of duplication. "Might be reused later" is not evidence.
- **Boundary rules** — no scope creep into untouched files, no test rewrites, no new dependencies, no commits/pushes (the orchestrator owns delivery).
- **Memory-persistence guidance with no-memory fallback** — instruct the host agent to persist recurring patterns via the host's memory mechanism (project memory, equivalents). **Fallback when host has no memory mechanism**: surface the pattern in the final summary under a `Recurring pattern observed:` line so the user can record it manually.

### R5 — Output format
Terse summary, one of:
- **Simplifications applied** — list each with `path:line` and one-line "what".
- **Flagged for review** — list each with `path:line`, candidate change, reason held back.
- **None warranted** — say so explicitly; do not invent work.

If a recurring pattern was persisted, append: `> Memory updated: <one-line description>`. If the host has no memory mechanism, the pattern appears under `Recurring pattern observed:` in the summary instead.

### R6 — Source-of-truth note as inline comment header
Co-located `AGENTS.md` was rejected during spec review (hosts only read tree-root `AGENTS.md`; a per-skill file would ship to three install destinations and be read by zero of them).

Instead, the skill file itself carries a comment header recording:
- **Source**: `/simplify` slash command (sidekick injection — outside this repo).
- **Drift policy**: (C) accept periodic re-sync as known cost.
- **Last sync**: 2026-05-02.

The comment must NOT corrupt YAML frontmatter parsing. Recommended placement: HTML comment immediately AFTER the closing `---` of the frontmatter and BEFORE the H1, e.g.:

```markdown
---
name: simplify
description: ...
---
<!-- Source: /simplify slash command (sidekick injection); Drift policy: (C) periodic re-sync; Last sync: 2026-05-02 -->

# Simplify: Code Review and Cleanup
```

Implementer should verify by inspecting one existing skill that this comment placement does not break frontmatter parsing on any of the three host runtimes.

## Out of Scope

- **Renaming the 27 dispatch sites** across 11 files — `agents-config-7bk.8`.
- **Deleting `src/user/.agents/agents/code-simplifier.md`** — `agents-config-7bk.7` (blocked on 7bk.8).
- **Editing the `/simplify` slash command body in sidekick** — drift policy is (C), not (B).
- **Updating `README.md` agent enumeration** — handled by 7bk.7.
- **Tests / typecheck / lint scaffolding** — docs-only repo; AC honored vacuously.
- **Per-skill `AGENTS.md` convention for other skills** — if the inline comment header proves valuable, generalize via a separate bead later. Not this one.
- **Drift mitigation revisit (option B — rewrite slash command to invoke skill)** — acknowledged as the only fix that actually eliminates drift, but defer to a separate bead.

## Design Notes

### Drift mitigation: option (C) chosen, surfaced via inline comment header
User chose (C) periodic re-sync. The original spec proposed a co-located `AGENTS.md` in the skill directory; spec review (wisp-czl) rejected this — hosts read `AGENTS.md` only at tree roots, not inside skill directories, so a per-skill `AGENTS.md` ships to three locations and is read by zero. Replaced with an HTML comment header at the top of the skill body, where it co-locates with the source it describes and is visible to anyone editing the skill.

### Capability detection: capability-shaped, not tool-named
Original spec said "if Agent tool available." That binds to Claude's tool naming and silently forces Codex/Gemini into the serial branch. Reworded to runtime-capability terms ("concurrent subagent dispatch in a single turn") with explicit examples for each host. Same pattern as `ralf-it/SKILL.md`.

### Memory: host-agnostic with explicit no-memory fallback
Skills don't carry agent-style `memory: project` frontmatter (host-agnostic). The body instructs the host to use its own memory mechanism. **What if the host has no memory at all?** Original spec was silent — patterns silently vanished. Now: surface the pattern in the final summary so the user can record it manually. Lossy but honest.

### Why the inline comment, not docs/specs/
Spec review suggested either inline comment OR `docs/specs/` one-pager. Inline wins because: (a) zero distance to the source it describes; (b) future agents editing the skill see the sync date without context-switching; (c) no risk of the docs/specs file orphaning if the skill moves.

### Already-extracted body and the R4 trap
The extracted body at `src/user/.agents/skills/simplify/SKILL.md` is the slash-command Phase-2 checklist verbatim and NOTHING ELSE. R4 disciplines (worktree scoping, apply/flag classification, ≥2 call-sites rule, boundary rules, memory guidance) are ABSENT. An implementer reading R2 ("preserved verbatim") plus inspecting the body could declare Phase 2 done and skip R4. The acceptance criteria explicitly call out the new `Operating Discipline` section to prevent this trap.

### install.sh dry-run AC pinned to all three tools
Original AC said "no collisions" without specifying which tools to install for. An implementer running `--tools=claude` could miss a Codex/Gemini collision. AC #7 now pins the flags: `--tools=claude,codex,gemini --plugins=beads`.

### Findings vs equivalence in serial mode
Spec review noted that serial Phase-2 execution can bias later reviewers with earlier findings. Acknowledged but not addressed — the cost of caveating the spec exceeds the practical impact at three review axes.

## Open Questions

None blocking. Two deferred concerns documented above (drift policy revisit; per-skill AGENTS.md generalization).

## Coordination

- **Blocks**: `agents-config-7bk.8` (the sweep cannot run until this skill exists).
- **Adjacent**: `agents-config-7bk.7` (now blocked on 7bk.8 → blocked transitively on this bead).
- **Edits confined** to `src/user/.agents/skills/simplify/`. No other source files mutate.

title: Agents fail to claim beads at start-of-work and fail to close parent epics; discovered-work guidance misses epic-sibling placement
design: Investigation bead — findings and proposal phase. Implementation would touch 6 files: brainstorm-bead.formula.toml, implement-feature.formula.toml, fix-bug.formula.toml, merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml, start-bead/SKILL.md, create-bead/SKILL.md, plus rules/beads.md for the invariants section.
notes:
# Findings (audit of src/plugins/beads/** and src/user/.agents/skills/**)

## GAP 1 — Claim at start-of-work is missing in the brainstorm path

**Where the claim happens today:**
| File | Step | Claims bead? | Claims parent? |
|------|------|--------------|----------------|
| src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/create-bead/SKILL.md | all | NO (correct — create is pure capture) | n/a |
| src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/start-bead/SKILL.md | Route A | delegates to implement-bead | via implement-bead |
| src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/start-bead/SKILL.md | Route B (trivial inline) | **NO — MISSING** | **NO — MISSING** |
| src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/start-bead/SKILL.md | Route C (brainstorm) | **NO — MISSING** | **NO — MISSING** |
| src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/brainstorm-bead.formula.toml | all steps (assess → finalize) | **NO — MISSING** | **NO — MISSING** |
| src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md | Step 3 (after pour) | YES | YES (one-line mention) |
| src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml | worktree step (step 2) | YES | YES |
| src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml | worktree step (step 3, after diagnose) | YES | brief mention |

**Root cause of observed lu3.4 bug:** The brainstorm-bead formula has zero claim step. start-bead Route C (the path for beads needing brainstorming) also doesn't claim. The child is worked on for the entire brainstorming arc (assess → discuss → write-spec → review → finalize) with status=open. Parent epic is never marked in_progress either.

## GAP 2 — Parent-epic closure is phrased as observation, not as a command

**Current housekeeping language (implement-feature, fix-bug, merge-and-cleanup):**
```
2. If all siblings in a parent epic are now closed, close the parent:
     bd show {{bead-id}}    # check for parent
     bd list --parent=<parent-id>  # check siblings
```

**Problems:**
- No concrete close command shown; agent has to infer it.
- No recursion up the parent chain (an epic may be a child of another epic).
- No handling for the 'all siblings closed but one deferred' edge case.
- The rules/beads.md one-liner 'After close → if all siblings closed, close parent recursively' is correct but not echoed in the formula steps.

**start-bead Route B (trivial inline)** says 'Close the bead when done' with no parent guidance.

## GAP 3 — Discovered-work guidance doesn't prefer epic-sibling placement

**Current guidance defaults to orphan + discovered-from dep:**
- rules/beads.md: 'Discovered work → create bead NOW with discovered-from:<parent-id> dep'
- implement-feature housekeeping step 4: `bd create ... && bd dep add <new-id> {{bead-id}} --type discovered-from`
- fix-bug housekeeping step 4: same pattern
- implement-bead skill 'Discovered work' note: 'create new beads immediately' — no placement guidance

**Missing rule:** if the work-in-progress is inside an epic AND the discovered work is logically a sibling subtask of that epic, create with `--parent <epic-id>` so it lands in the epic, not as an orphan with only a dep link.

create-bead skill DOES document the `--parent <epic-id>` option but only in the 'child tasks under an epic' use case. Nothing tells discovering agents to prefer that placement.

---

# Proposed enhancements (concrete, reviewable)

## E1 — brainstorm-bead formula: add explicit claim step
New first step `claim` (before `assess`):
```toml
[[steps]]
id = "claim"
title = "Claim bead and parent epic"
description = """
Mark this bead and any parent-chain epics as in_progress before any work.

  bd update {{bead-id}} --status in_progress

  # Walk parent chain; mark each ancestor epic in_progress
  PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.parent // empty')
  while [ -n "$PARENT" ]; do
    bd update $PARENT --status in_progress
    PARENT=$(bd show $PARENT --json | jq -r '.parent // empty')
  done
"""
```
Mark `assess` as `needs = ["claim"]`.

## E2 — start-bead Route B (trivial inline): claim + close with parent walk
Add to Route B description: 'Before starting: claim bead + walk parent chain. After finishing: close bead + walk parent chain to close any epic whose remaining children are all closed.'

## E3 — Promote parent-epic closure from observation to command
In housekeeping steps of implement-feature, fix-bug, merge-and-cleanup, replace the fuzzy paragraph with:
```bash
# Recursive parent-epic close
PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.parent // empty')
while [ -n "$PARENT" ]; do
  OPEN=$(bd list --parent=$PARENT --status=open --json | jq 'length')
  [ "$OPEN" = "0" ] || break
  bd close $PARENT --reason "All children closed"
  PARENT=$(bd show $PARENT --json | jq -r '.parent // empty')
done
```

## E4 — Discovered-work: prefer epic-sibling placement
Update create-bead skill Step 2 with a new sub-section 'Placement of discovered work':
- If you're inside an in-progress bead that has a parent epic AND the new work is logically a sibling subtask of that epic → `bd create ... --parent <epic-id>`
- Otherwise → orphan + `bd dep add <new-id> <current-id> --type discovered-from`

Update implement-feature and fix-bug housekeeping step 4 to reflect this:
```bash
PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.parent // empty')
if [ -n "$PARENT" ] && <is-sibling-subtask>; then
  bd create "..." -t <type> -p <prio> --parent $PARENT
else
  bd create "..." -t <type> -p <prio>
  bd dep add <new-id> {{bead-id}} --type discovered-from
fi
```

## E5 — rules/beads.md: crystallize invariants
Replace the three-line 'Parent/child workflow' stub with a named 'Parent-chain invariants' section that states:
1. Child claim → walk parent chain, set each ancestor epic to in_progress.
2. Child close → walk parent chain, close each ancestor epic whose remaining children are all closed.
3. Discovered work under an in-progress epic → create with `--parent <epic-id>` (sibling placement) when it's logically a sibling subtask; otherwise orphan + discovered-from.

---

# Status
Findings complete. Proposed enhancements documented. Awaiting user review before implementation.
