title: wait-for-pr-comments: tune Phase 4 timing windows from real-world Copilot data
description:
Empirical timing data gathered from 13 PRs in scotthamilton77/claude-code-sidekick (PRs #160, #168, #173–#183) covering 18 copilot_work_started events. Repo has auto-Copilot-review + auto-rereview-on-push enabled at the org/repo level — every review_requested event is server-generated within seconds of a push.

KEY TIMINGS (push → review_requested → copilot_work_started):

| Phase                                  | push→request | request→work | push→work    |
|----------------------------------------|--------------|--------------|--------------|
| Initial review (1st on PR)             | 34–164s      | 22–39s       | 24–193s      |
| Re-review after prior COMMENTED review | 2–7s         | 22–37s       | 24–44s       |
| Long quiescent / draft resumption      | up to 10hrs  | ~30s         | up to 10hrs  |

Follow-up case (what Phase 4 cares about): tight cluster, 24–44s total push→work_started.

Iteration-depth observations:
- 2nd review on a PR: 4 samples, push→request = 2, 3, 3, 7s
- 3rd review on a PR: 1 sample (PR #183), push→request = 3s
- 4th review on a PR: 0 samples in 6-week dataset — auto-rereview stops after some N. Mechanism unknown (hypotheses: hard iteration cap, diff-similarity skip, rate limit). Out of scope.

PROPOSED FIXES TO wait-for-pr-comments:

1. Phase 4 (poll-copilot-rereview-start.sh) currently polls 3 × 10s = 30s window. Real upper bound for follow-up push→work is ~44s. The 30s window is on the edge. Extend to 60s minimum (6 × 10s polls).

2. Add an explicit floor sleep before the first poll. Polling 1s after push will always miss; lower bound is push+24s. Recommend a 20s pre-sleep.

3. Proactively detect repo-level auto-rereview is configured (gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}) and adjust report wording — users don't need to manually re-request. The unknown 'sometimes stops' condition is out of scope; the script should still correctly report 'no_rereview_started' rather than a stale-cache false 'review found'.

4. Combined with sidekick-aga3 (stale poll bug), holistic fix for poll-copilot-review.sh in --skip-request-check mode: filter reviews by submitted_at > last_commit_pushed_at (or > work_started_at if known). Polling loop continues up to the 10-minute budget until a fresh review lands or budget exhausts — never returns a cached review as 'fresh found'.

Acceptance: Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass. PR #183 today (4th push, no auto-rereview) should produce 'no_rereview_started' in the correct 60s window, not a false 'review found' off stale cache.

title: Cleanup: branches, worktrees, bead
description:

1. Delete remote branch (if not auto-deleted by GitHub):
     gh pr view 24 --json headRefName | jq -r '.headRefName'
     gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/git/refs/heads/<branch> -X DELETE

2. Delete local branch:

     git worktree list
     git worktree remove <path>   # only if a worktree existed

4. Close the bead:
     bd close {{bead-id}} --reason "Merged PR #24"

5. Walk the parent chain; close each ancestor epic whose remaining
   children are all closed (stops at the first ancestor that still
   has any non-closed children, or when there is no parent):

     PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
     while [ -n "$PARENT" ]; do
       NON_CLOSED=$(bd list --parent="$PARENT" --json | jq '[.[] | select(.status != "closed")] | length')
       [ "$NON_CLOSED" = "0" ] || break
       bd close "$PARENT" --reason "All children closed"

     done

6. Burn this wisp:

       bd dep add "$NEW" {{bead-id}} --type discovered-from
     fi

   have been on the parent epic's original plan, if we'd thought of
   it? Yes → sibling (--parent). No → orphan + discovered-from.

title: Expand brainstorm-bead scope: AC mechanical/human classification, brainstorm-time knobs (auto-mergeable, review-requirements), human-only follow-up beads
description:
Expand brainstorm-bead so the brainstorm phase captures policy decisions: which acceptance criteria are machine-verifiable vs require human judgment, whether the bead should auto-merge, and what depth of code review applies. Wire downstream formulas (merge-and-cleanup, implement-feature, fix-bug) to honor those decisions.

Full design: `docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md` §4 (Brainstorm-bead expansion), §4.1 (AC classification), §4.2 (Policy knob labels), §4.3 (Human follow-up beads + two-gate model + idempotent finalize).

## Scope

### R1 — AC classification

- AC lines are tagged `[m]` (mechanical) or `[h]` (human)
- Canonical line parser regex + fallback: see architecture doc §4.1
- bead-spec personas (per 7bk.3) draft AC tags during write-spec; user reviews and may override

### R2 — Policy knob labels

- `auto-mergeable` — presence label; when set, merge fires automatically once both gates clear (PR-state clean-check + source-bead closure gate-step). See architecture doc §4.3.
- `review-level:none|light|standard|deep` — gates downstream review depth per the skip matrix in architecture doc §4.2.

Reader contract for label vocabulary (collision rules, parameterized prefixes): see architecture doc §4.2.

### R3 — Human follow-up beads + merge-gate child

For each `[h]` AC line, the `finalize` step spawns a child bead per architecture doc §4.3:
- `--parent <source-bead-id>`, `--type task`, label `human`, NOT labeled `implementation-ready`
- Description carries the canonical close-without-verify warning text
- A human closing this bead WITHOUT applying the `verified-by-human` label does NOT satisfy the merge gate (per architecture doc §4.3 + R5 below).

Additionally, `finalize` files a single `merge-{source-id}` child (architecture doc §4.3):
- `--parent <source-bead-id>`, `--type task`, `--title "[Merge gate] <source-title>"`
- Description: "Closes when the merge-and-cleanup formula's merge step completes."
- Label `merge-gate` (so the gate-step can identify it)
- NOT labeled `human` (system-managed, not human-attention)
- NOT labeled `implementation-ready`

The merge-{source-id} child closes as the FINAL action of merge-and-cleanup's merge step. Its presence ensures the source bead's I2 close-walk does not fire prematurely on the auto-merge path.

### R4 — brainstorm-bead.formula.toml changes

- `write-spec` step: instruct agent to draft AC with `[m]/[h]` tags
- NEW `classify-and-policy` step inserted between `write-spec` and `ralf-spec-review`. Re-reads AC tags, proposes auto-mergeable + review-level, presents recommendations together for single user confirmation cycle, stamps agreed labels (with prior-label removal for mutually-exclusive sets like `review-level:*`). Per architecture doc §4.2 combination warnings, the step also WARNS and requires explicit user override when:
  - `review-level:none` AND `auto-mergeable` AND zero `[h]` AC (no verification gate of any kind)
  - `review-level:none` on a bead whose title or description mentions auth/token/password/permission/secret keywords
  - `auto-mergeable` on a bead with any `[h]` AC line (the auto-merge bypass cannot fire if `[h]` children block the gate; combination is logically inconsistent)
- `ralf-spec-review` scope widened: heuristically reviews classification + knob choices for plausibility
- `finalize` step is **idempotent** per architecture doc §4.3:
  - Lists current open follow-up children of source bead (filter by `human` label + parent)
  - For each `[h]` AC line: create matching child if absent; leave existing alone
  - For each existing follow-up whose AC line is no longer `[h]` in current spec: close with reason "AC re-classified to [m] or removed; follow-up no longer needed"
  - Creates `merge-{source-id}` child if absent
  - Re-running with unchanged AC is a no-op
  - Stamps `implementation-ready` label only after follow-ups are reconciled

### R5 — Consumer formula changes

- `merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml`:
  - MUST be `phase = "liquid"` (vapor cannot persist across the [h] verification window)
  - Gate-step blocks until source-bead closes AND every `[h]` follow-up child carries the `verified-by-human` label. A `closed` follow-up without `verified-by-human` does NOT satisfy the gate; gate-step flags-human with a note about the missing label.
  - PR-state clean-check (Copilot threads zero, human threads zero, CI green, 5-minute quiet window) — gate 1 of the two-gate model
  - merge step closes the `merge-{source-id}` child as its FINAL action (ensures I2 close-walk on source fires correctly)
- `implement-feature.formula.toml` — gate steps (code-review, simplify, verify, adversarial-codex) self-skip per the source bead's `review-level:*` label. Skip matrix: see architecture doc §4.2.
- `fix-bug.formula.toml` — same `review-level:*` self-skip pattern applied to its review/simplify/verify gate steps.

### R6 — Documentation updates

- `src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md`: extend the Bead Lifecycle and Labels table with `auto-mergeable`, `review-level:*`, `verified-by-human`, and `merge-gate` rows; note that [h]-spawned human follow-ups are children of the source bead and participate in the I2 close walk; cross-reference architecture doc §4.1 for canonical AC parser
- `src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/delivery.md`: note the auto-mergeable two-gate semantics + gate-step mechanism that blocks merge on open [h] follow-up children OR follow-ups missing `verified-by-human`

## Out of Scope

- Beads with ONLY [h] AC (spec-only, no implementation)
- AC tag aliases (`[Mechanical]`, `[H]`, etc.) — strict regex only
- Migration of existing in-flight beads — defaults handle them transparently
- Shared AC-parsing library — each consumer inlines the canonical pattern
- New formula primitive for conditional steps — pattern (c), step self-skips by reading source bead labels, is sufficient
- Recursive human follow-ups — they are leaf tasks

## Dependencies

- 7bk.3 — bead-spec personas (provides the agent that drafts AC tags). Velocity dep, not correctness; could land first if 7bk.3 stalls.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [m] `brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` contains new classify-and-policy step inserted between write-spec and ralf-spec-review with `Agent: bead-assessor` (or bead-specwriter) dispatch directive
- [m] `classify-and-policy` step warns on dangerous combinations (review-level:none + auto-mergeable + zero [h]; review-level:none on security-keyword beads; auto-mergeable + [h] AC) and requires explicit user override
- [m] `brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` write-spec step instructs agent to tag AC with `[m]/[h]`, defaults untagged lines to `[m]`, and documents the canonical regex parser
- [m] `brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` finalize step is idempotent: re-runs reconcile follow-up children to current AC (create new, close obsolete, leave matching alone)
- [m] `finalize` step spawns one child bead per `[h]` AC line (parent=source, label=human, type=task, NOT labeled `implementation-ready`)
- [m] `finalize` step files a `merge-{source-id}` child (parent=source, label=merge-gate, NOT labeled human, NOT labeled implementation-ready)
- [m] `merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml` is `phase = "liquid"` (persists across [h] verification window)
- [m] `merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml` gate-step blocks on source bead closure AND every [h] child carries `verified-by-human` label
- [m] `merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml` adds PR-state check-cleanliness step (zero open Copilot threads, zero open human threads, CI green, 5-minute quiet window)
- [m] `merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml` merge step closes merge-{source-id} child as its FINAL action
- [m] `implement-feature.formula.toml` gate steps self-skip per source bead `review-level:*` per architecture doc §4.2 skip matrix
- [m] `fix-bug.formula.toml` applies same `review-level:*` self-skip pattern

- [m] `src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/delivery.md` describes auto-mergeable two-gate semantics + verified-by-human requirement
- [m] Build passes
- [h] Smoke test 1: brainstorm fresh bead with mixed `[m]/[h]` AC; confirm `[h]` lines spawn child beads with parent=source, label=human, no `implementation-ready` label; confirm merge-{source-id} child created
- [h] Smoke test 2: confirm `merge-and-cleanup` halts (gate-step blocks) when at least one `[h]` follow-up child is open OR closed without `verified-by-human`; closing all children with `verified-by-human` clears the gate
- [h] Smoke test 3: confirm `auto-mergeable` label bypasses explicit human auth gate ONLY when both gates clear; on timeout or dirty PR, human auth still required
- [h] Smoke test 4: confirm `review-level:*` labels (none, light, standard, deep) correctly route through implement-feature per the skip matrix
- [h] Smoke test 5: confirm finalize idempotency — re-run brainstorm-bead with [h] AC line re-classified to [m]; confirm corresponding follow-up child closes automatically

title: Production shell driver for autonomous bead processing (replaces run-queue skill)
description:
Production shell driver for autonomous bead processing — replaces the run-queue skill once functional parity is reached.

Depends on 7bk.9 (per-stage claude -p architecture).

## Why

The current run-queue skill polls bd for implementation-ready beads in a single Claude session. Two problems: (1) unbounded context growth across many beads in one session; (2) it dispatches implement-bead, which under the per-stage redesign (7bk.9) needs to be replaced by per-stage claude -p invocations.

A shell driver outside Claude Code can poll bd, spawn `claude -p` per ready stage, respect the molecule DAG, and recycle resources cleanly without unbounded context growth.

## Brainstorm-time questions to resolve

- **Concurrency model:** single driver instance vs. multiple? Per-bead vs. per-stage parallelism? Cap on concurrent claude -p processes?
- **Foreign-CLI rate limits:** if multiple green-loop stages run concurrently, Codex/Gemini rate limits apply. Per-driver concurrency limit on foreign-eyes calls?
- **Worktree contention:** all worktrees go to .claude/worktrees/. Git lock contention on shared .git/ at scale?
- **Driver lifecycle:** cron, daemon, on-demand invocation? Crash recovery semantics? Stuck-detection (stage open >N hours)?
- **bd database access:** concurrent updates to .beads/*.db from multiple driver instances. Locking? Single-writer enforced?
- **Mid-pipeline main-branch drift:** when bead A merges while bead B is mid green-loop, B's worktree is behind main. Rebase protocol?
- **`claude -p` start failure handling:** API down / quota / auth — exponential backoff per-bead-per-stage? When does the driver give up and flag-human?
- **Removal of run-queue skill:** at what point does this driver replace user-facing run-queue functionality? Coordinate with 7bk.9.

## Acceptance Criteria (placeholder — refine at brainstorm)

- Driver runs continuously, polls bd, spawns claude -p per ready stage per molecule
- Concurrency model documented + enforced
- Crash recovery semantics defined
- run-queue skill removed once driver achieves functional parity
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

title: Per-step claude -p orchestration: redesign bead pipeline (replace run-queue skill, rewrite implement-bead, define 7-stage feature workflow)
description:
Implement the per-stage `claude -p` execution model defined in `docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md` (canonical target architecture). Replaces the run-queue skill's single-session polling model and the prior implement-bead-as-molecule-orchestrator model.

## Scope (work owned by THIS bead)

1. Rewrite `implement-bead` skill — execute ONE stage of a molecule and exit; not "drive the entire molecule." Per-stage orchestrator behavior. Reads the appropriate stage step-bead, dispatches subagents/skills/slash commands per the stage's spec, persists state-out, exits.

2. Add `/implement-bead` slash command wrapper at `src/plugins/beads/.claude/commands/implement-bead.md` — thin wrapper taking `$ARGUMENTS` (bead-id) and invoking the implement-bead skill. Both invocation paths supported: interactive Claude sessions reach the skill via discovery; the shell driver's `claude -p` reaches it deterministically via the slash command.

3. Rewrite `start-bead` skill — Route A's hand-off destination changes to the shell driver (was: run-queue). Routing logic otherwise preserved. Route D (resolve-human-bead) is added by 7bk.13.

4. Add minimal test shell driver at `scripts/bead-driver-test.sh` — `bd ready --label implementation-ready --json` query loop + spawn `claude -p` per ready stage. Mark `run-queue` skill DEPRECATED but do NOT delete it; deletion happens in 7bk.11 (production driver) once it achieves functional parity.

5. Update `implement-feature.formula.toml` — restructure step list to match the architecture's 8 role-named stages (`preflight`, `red-tests`, `green-loop`, `quality-sweep`, `verify-ac`, `create-pr`, `review-cycle`, `merge-or-handoff`). Each step body becomes "orchestrator-tier instructions for one stage's claude -p" rather than wrapped-subagent instructions.

6. Update `fix-bug.formula.toml` — same 8-stage structure plus a `diagnose` stage at position 2 (between `preflight` and `red-tests`). The diagnose stage runs `superpowers:systematic-debugging` + `superpowers:root-cause-tracing` and produces a root-cause note before any tests are written.

7. Create `bead-implementor.md` agent at `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/`. Specs: see architecture doc §6 (Agent catalog).

8. Document worktree-path label lifecycle in `implement-bead/SKILL.md` — orchestrator owns worktree creation in `preflight` stage and stamps the `worktree-path-*` label per the encoding spec in architecture doc §5.3. Subsequent stages read and inject.

9. Document `project-config.toml` schema per architecture doc §5.1; provide an example file at the repo root.

10. End-to-end smoke test against scratch fixture project — `scripts/smoke/setup.sh` creates fresh `/tmp/bead-pipeline-smoke-<timestamp>/`, initializes with `bd init`, creates trivial source + test, populates `project-config.toml`. `scripts/bead-driver-test.sh` processes a seed bead through all stages. Smoke acceptance: all stages execute and exit cleanly, molecule completes, scratch project's PR materializes; the agents-config bd database remains untouched.

11. Slash-command discovery from worktree subdirectories verified by smoke test (Claude Code's `.claude/` walk-up resolves to the worktree's checked-in commands).

## Out of scope (covered elsewhere)

- Production driver (cron, daemon, concurrency): 7bk.11
- Single-context mode alternative: 76r
- Brainstorm-bead policy capture (AC classification, knobs): 7bk.12
- Per-step Model:/Effort: directives mechanism: 7bk.14
- Per-stage default model/effort table: 7bk.15
- A/B testing methodology: 7bk.16
- RALF-IT foreign-eyes hard-fail: z7a
- Decomposed bead-spec personas + brainstorm formula rewiring: 7bk.3
- Skill-folklore sweep (`bd human <id>`): gcg

## Acceptance Criteria

- All scope items 1-11 implemented per architecture doc
- End-to-end smoke test passes in isolated `/tmp/` scratch project
- Stage idempotent re-entry verified for green-loop (kill mid-iteration, re-spawn, resume from `RALF-IT-ITER` marker)
- Pinned UUIDv5 namespace `27ece4fd-4a06-49bf-a921-bf07ecb0dc10` used for session-id derivation
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

## Dependencies

- ✓ 1ck (closed: *-developer agents removed)
- ✓ 7bk.2 (closed: superseded by this bead)
- (peer) 7bk.7 — code-simplifier agent deletion

title: Confirm bead is brainstormed and restate the implementation plan
description:
Verify that bead {{bead-id}} has been through brainstorm-bead and that
the spec exists in the bead. NEVER invoke superpowers:brainstorming —
the bead description IS the plan.

Read bead and labels:

  bd show {{bead-id}}
  bd label list {{bead-id}}

== HAPPY PATH (both `brainstormed` AND `implementation-ready` labels present) ==

1. Read the bead description and acceptance criteria.
2. Restate the implementation plan in 1-3 sentences to prove understanding
   (e.g. "I will modify <files> to <do X> so that <AC pass>").
3. Mark this step done. The next step (worktree) runs.

DO NOT call `bd update {{bead-id}} --acceptance` or `--notes` —
brainstorm-bead already wrote the spec; rewriting it would destroy it.

== MISSING-LABEL PATH (either label absent) ==

Aborting from either branch below means: leave this `confirm` step
OPEN (do not close it), do NOT call `bd mol burn` from within this
step, and print this one-line follow-up:

  Molecule paused at confirm. Run brainstorm-bead on this bead and re-attempt, or `bd mol burn <mol-id>` to discard.

Leave the disposition (re-attempt or burn) to the operator (or to
run-queue policy).

Detect execution mode from your own dispatch context:

- AUTONOMOUS mode (you were dispatched by run-queue, or any
  context where no human is at the keyboard):
  - Escalate the bead so a human can address it:
      bd human {{bead-id}}
  - Note in escalation: "missing brainstormed/implementation-ready labels; recommend running brainstorm-bead before implementation."
  - Do NOT prompt — there is no operator to answer.
  - Then abort (per the shared abort mechanism above).

- INTERACTIVE mode (start-bead routed you here, or a human ran
  `bd mol pour` directly and is in conversation with you):
  - Print a clear warning: "Bead {{bead-id}} is missing brainstormed/implementation-ready labels — it has not been through brainstorm-bead. Implementation may run on an incomplete spec."
  - Ask the user:
    (a) Abort and route the bead through start-bead/brainstorm-bead first.
    (b) Proceed with implementation using whatever is in the bead now.
  - On (a): abort (per the shared abort mechanism above).
  - On (b): proceed to worktree.
close_reason: Bead confirmed brainstormed + implementation-ready; plan restated

title: Refactor for clarity (refactor phase)
description:
Clean up the implementation without changing observable behavior.
Tests must remain green throughout.

Skill: code-simplifier:code-simplifier (run as subagent)

Look for:
- Duplication (three similar lines is a smell; two is acceptable)
- Names that require a comment to understand
- Logic that could be expressed more simply
- Dead code

Re-run tests after every change. If tests break, you changed behavior.
Undo the change and try again.

This step is NOT about adding features or improving performance.
It is about making the code readable by the next person (who is you,
in six months, having forgotten everything).

title: Housekeeping: close, remember, file discovered work
description:
Complete the lifecycle and preserve context for future sessions.

1. Close the bead:
     bd close {{bead-id}} --reason "Implemented and PR merged"

2. Walk the parent chain; close each ancestor epic whose remaining
   children are all closed (stops at the first ancestor that still
   has any non-closed children, or when there is no parent):

     PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
     while [ -n "$PARENT" ]; do
       NON_CLOSED=$(bd list --parent="$PARENT" --json | jq '[.[] | select(.status != "closed")] | length')
       [ "$NON_CLOSED" = "0" ] || break
       bd close "$PARENT" --reason "All children closed"
       PARENT=$(bd show "$PARENT" --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
     done

3. Record any non-obvious decisions or hard-won insights:
     bd remember "insight about this system or pattern"
     bd decision record --title="Why we chose X over Y"

4. File any work discovered during implementation. Prefer epic-sibling
   placement over orphan-with-dep:

     PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')

     # Decide placement via the sibling test (see rules/beads.md I3):
     # would this work have been on the parent epic's original plan, if
     # we'd thought of it? Yes → sibling. No → orphan + discovered-from.
     IS_SIBLING_SUBTASK=false  # set to true only when the answer is yes

     if [ -n "$PARENT" ] && [ "$IS_SIBLING_SUBTASK" = true ]; then
       # Create INSIDE the parent epic as a sibling:
       bd create "Found: ..." -t "<type>" -p "<prio>" --parent "$PARENT"
     else
       # Otherwise create as an orphan and link with discovered-from:
       NEW=$(bd create "Found: ..." -t "<type>" -p "<prio>" --json | jq -r '.id')
       bd dep add "$NEW" {{bead-id}} --type discovered-from
     fi

   Sibling test (see rules/beads.md I3): would this discovered work
   have been on the parent epic's original plan, if we'd thought of
   it? Yes → sibling (--parent). No → orphan + discovered-from.

5. Burn this wisp (if running as vapor):
     bd mol burn <this-mol-id>
close_reason: Bead b7s closed (no parent); two memories persisted (per-skill-agents-md-is-dead-file; b7s-simplify-drift-policy); no discovered work to file

title: Set up isolated git worktree
description:
Invoke the using-git-worktrees skill to create an isolated branch.

Skill: superpowers:using-git-worktrees

- Branch from main (never from another feature branch)
- Verify you are IN the worktree directory before writing any code
- Confirm no leftover state from other work

Then claim the bead and walk the parent chain:

  bd update {{bead-id}} --status in_progress

  # Walk parent chain; mark each ancestor epic in_progress
  PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
  while [ -n "$PARENT" ]; do
    bd update "$PARENT" --status in_progress
    PARENT=$(bd show "$PARENT" --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
  done

If `implement-bead` already claimed the bead before pouring this
formula, the first `bd update` is a no-op — re-running it is safe.

title: Delete code-simplifier agent; rewire completion-gate to invoke /simplify directly
notes:
## Update (agents-config-7bk.8 brainstorm session, 2026-05-02)

**Architecture flip — this bead's scope has narrowed.**

Original Path C ("Have completion-gate step 3 invoke /simplify directly via the orchestrator") is OBSOLETE. New plan:
- agents-config-b7s creates a portable `simplify` skill (ported from the /simplify command body)
- agents-config-7bk.8 rewrites `src/user/.claude/rules/completion-gate.md` step 3 (and ~10 other dispatch sites across the codebase) to invoke the new `simplify` skill — uniform invocation, no orchestrator-tier-only guard
- This bead (7bk.7) now ONLY deletes the broken agent file at `src/user/.agents/agents/code-simplifier.md`

**New dependency**: agents-config-7bk.7 now depends on agents-config-7bk.8 (the sweep must complete before the agent file deletion, otherwise stale dispatches pointing at a non-existent agent would proliferate).

**Original notes (preserved for context, but now partially obsolete):**

---

## Discovery (verbatim from session 5385d9fc)

1. /simplify is a slash command, NOT a skill. It lives outside this repo (installed via a plugin or sidekick injection — not present in src/). When invoked, its body is loaded into the session as a prompt.
2. The /simplify command is itself a parallel-subagent dispatcher. Its Phase 2 explicitly says: "Use the Agent tool to launch all three agents concurrently in a single message" — three reviewers (reuse, quality, efficiency), then aggregate, then apply fixes.
3. The code-simplifier agent at src/user/.agents/agents/code-simplifier.md is BROKEN. It instructs itself to "invoke the bundled /simplify skill … via the Skill tool with skill name simplify" — but no such skill exists. It points at a phantom dependency.
4. Subagents CANNOT invoke slash commands (those are user/orchestrator-initiated), so a simple "fix the reference" patch will NOT work.
5. completion-gate.md step 3 currently calls the code-simplifier agent.

## Decision (Path C) — SUPERSEDED, see Update above

Delete the code-simplifier agent layer entirely. Have completion-gate step 3 invoke /simplify directly via the orchestrator (the orchestrator CAN run slash commands).

## What must be preserved (currently encoded in the agent — needs a new home)

- Worktree scoping (operate in the supplied worktree path, not the main tree)
- Behavior-preserving discipline (apply-vs-flag classification, "no abstractions without ≥ 2 call sites")
- Project memory persistence (.claude/agent-memory/code-simplifier/ recurring-pattern capture)
- Boundary rules (no scope creep, no new dependencies, no test rewrites, no commits/pushes)

→ NEW HOME: agents-config-b7s ports these into the `simplify` skill body.

## Files involved (revised)

- src/user/.agents/agents/code-simplifier.md — DELETE (this bead)
- src/user/.claude/rules/completion-gate.md — handled by 7bk.8, not this bead
- /simplify command source — handled by b7s
- Any other rules/skills referencing code-simplifier agent — handled by 7bk.8 sweep

## Suggested execution steps (revised)

1. Verify 7bk.8 sweep is complete (`bd show 7bk.8` status=closed).
2. Confirm `grep -rn "code-simplifier" src/` returns empty (the sweep should have made it so).
3. Delete src/user/.agents/agents/code-simplifier.md.
4. Run completion gate on the change.

## Relationship to agents-config-7bk.2

Phase 2 (7bk.2) of the agent fleet epic is in flight. Original coordination still applies — 7bk.2's formula updates land independently.

title: Phase 2: bead-implementor agent + formula rewiring
description:
Phase 2 of agents-config-7bk. Wires the new specialized agent fleet into the bead pipeline by introducing the bead-implementor agent, augmenting implement-bead with worktree lifetime ownership, and rewiring the implement-feature and fix-bug formulas to dispatch the right agents for review/implement/verify and to invoke /simplify directly (orchestrator-side) for the simplify gate.

## Summary

Add a single named "implementor" agent (bead-implementor) that subagents are dispatched as for write-tests, implement, and diagnose steps. Move worktree creation/teardown from per-step responsibility into the implement-bead orchestrator, persisting the worktree path on the molecule via a label so every dispatched subagent receives it. Update both implementation formulas to use current agent names (post Phase 1 + post *-developer cleanup), and convert the simplify step from subagent dispatch to orchestrator-direct /simplify invocation.

## Background

Originally specced 2026-04-26 alongside the dynamic-dove plan. The world has shifted since:
- *-developer agents were deleted (commit 92fdfda; bead 1ck closed); the dynamic-dove "preload domain skills by subject area" justification is gone.
- code-debugger.md was deleted in the same commit; superpowers:systematic-debugging + root-cause-tracing replace it.
- code-reviewer was merged into quality-reviewer (commit 827ef3e).
- bead-verifier was delivered in Phase 1 (7bk.1) but is not yet wired into any formula.
- Formulas still reference stale agent paths (superpowers:code-reviewer, code-simplifier:code-simplifier).
- DISCOVERY (session 5385d9fc): /simplify is a slash COMMAND (not a skill), and the code-simplifier agent is broken (points at a phantom skill). Subagents cannot invoke slash commands, so the simplify step must be orchestrator-direct, not subagent-dispatched. The actual deletion of the code-simplifier agent is tracked separately in agents-config-7bk.7 (peer cleanup bead, non-blocking).

This bead consolidates the surviving Phase 2 work: add bead-implementor as the implementing/debugging muscle, wire bead-verifier into the verify gate, fix stale agent references, centralize worktree lifecycle in the orchestrator, and convert the simplify step to orchestrator-direct invocation.

## Requirements

1. **CREATE `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/bead-implementor.md`** — minimal "implementor" agent.
   - Frontmatter: name=bead-implementor, model=sonnet, color=blue, tools=Read+Edit+Write+Grep+Glob+Bash, memory=project.
   - skills: [superpowers:test-driven-development, writing-unit-tests, testing-anti-patterns, superpowers:using-git-worktrees, superpowers:verification-before-completion, superpowers:systematic-debugging, superpowers:root-cause-tracing]
   - Body: cd into the worktree path passed by the orchestrator (via prompt — derived from molecule label, see #2), follow preloaded TDD methodology for write-tests/implement steps, follow preloaded debugging skills for diagnose steps. Never declare done without verification evidence. NO domain-skill preloading machinery — just the fixed skills list above.

2. **AUGMENT `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md`** — orchestrator owns the worktree for the bead's full lifetime.
   - On the FIRST dispatched step that needs a worktree (typically write-tests for features, diagnose for bugs), the orchestrator invokes superpowers:using-git-worktrees, captures the path, and stamps a label on the molecule of the form `worktree-path-<sanitized>` where slashes in the path are replaced by `__` (decode on read).
   - On every subsequent subagent dispatch, the orchestrator parses the label off the molecule and injects the decoded worktree path into the subagent's prompt.
   - Worktree teardown happens in the merge-and-cleanup formula (out of scope for this bead beyond noting the contract — merge-and-cleanup already exists).
   - Document the label format and parse/encode helpers in the SKILL.md so future agents can interpret the convention.

3. **UPDATE `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml`:**
   - Replace `Agent: superpowers:code-reviewer` → `Agent: quality-reviewer` (review step).
   - Convert simplify step from `Agent: code-simplifier:code-simplifier` to ORCHESTRATOR-DIRECT: the step body instructs the orchestrator to invoke the `/simplify` slash command directly (no Agent: line), since /simplify itself dispatches three parallel subagents internally. Document this in the step text so future readers understand why simplify is structured differently from review/verify.
   - Wire `Agent: bead-implementor` into write-tests and implement steps (replacing whatever generic dispatch is currently there).
   - Wire `Agent: bead-verifier` into the verify step.

4. **UPDATE `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml`:**
   - Same review and simplify changes as #3 (review = quality-reviewer; simplify = orchestrator-direct /simplify).
   - Wire `Agent: bead-implementor` into the diagnose, write-tests, and implement steps (bead-implementor's preloaded systematic-debugging + root-cause-tracing skills cover diagnose).
   - Wire `Agent: bead-verifier` into the verify step.

5. **DROP from original spec:** no longer modify code-debugger.md (file deleted in 92fdfda; debugging skills now live as preloaded skills on bead-implementor per #1).

6. **End-to-end smoke test:** push a trivial bead through run-queue and confirm the new dispatches fire correctly — bead-implementor runs in the persisted worktree, bead-verifier reports exit codes, quality-reviewer runs in the review step, and the simplify step's orchestrator-direct /simplify invocation completes (or is skipped cleanly if 7bk.7 hasn't landed yet — confirm graceful behavior either way). Worktree label stamps on the molecule and parses correctly across step boundaries.

## Out of Scope

- Domain-skill preloading on bead-implementor (1ck closed; *-developer skills not built).
- Deletion of src/user/.agents/agents/code-simplifier.md and rewriting of completion-gate.md step 3 — tracked in agents-config-7bk.7.
- Editing the /simplify command body (also 7bk.7 territory if that bead chooses to add a Phase 0 discipline section).
- Teardown logic in merge-and-cleanup beyond confirming the existing flow can read the label-stored path.
- Changes to brainstorm-bead.formula.toml (no agent dispatch in brainstorming).
- Updating tech-lead.md or any other surviving agent (Phase 1 territory or already done).
- Replacing the existing `for-bead-<bead-id>` molecule label convention (orthogonal; worktree-path label is additive).

## Design Notes

- **Why a single bead-implementor instead of multiple specialized "implementor" variants:** the original dynamic-dove rationale was domain-skill preloading. With *-developer skills not built, there's no useful axis on which to specialize. One named role keeps formula entries traceable and gives a clear seam if domain preloading is ever revived.
- **Why label storage for worktree path:** labels are lifecycle-neutral, queryable via existing bd CLI, and don't entangle the molecule's notes/description (which formulas may overwrite). Sanitization is a known cost; the alternative (mol notes) gets clobbered by step logic that uses --notes.
- **Why bead-implementor handles diagnose:** option (a) from the brainstorm Q3 — preloading systematic-debugging + root-cause-tracing on a single agent is cheaper than building a parallel bead-debugger and gives the formula one consistent dispatch target across feature/bug pipelines.
- **Why simplify is orchestrator-direct, not subagent-dispatched:** /simplify is a slash command (not a skill) and itself dispatches three parallel reviewer subagents internally. Subagents cannot invoke slash commands, so wrapping /simplify in an "Agent: code-simplifier" dispatch is structurally impossible. The orchestrator (the agent driving the molecule) IS the only layer that can run /simplify, so the formula step body instructs it directly.
- **Worktree label encoding:** `worktree-path-<path-with-slashes-replaced-by-double-underscore>`. Decode by reversing the replacement. Example: `/Users/scott/foo/bar` → `worktree-path-__Users__scott__foo__bar`. Skills/agents that read this MUST parse from the well-known prefix.
- **Relationship with 7bk.7:** 7bk.7 (delete code-simplifier agent + rewrite completion-gate step 3) is a peer cleanup, not a blocker. 7bk.2's formula updates avoid referencing the doomed agent regardless of when 7bk.7 lands. They are independent.

## Acceptance Criteria

- src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/bead-implementor.md exists with frontmatter and body matching requirement #1.
- src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md documents and implements the worktree-label lifecycle (create-on-first-need, stamp, parse-and-inject on subsequent dispatches).
- implement-feature.formula.toml and fix-bug.formula.toml reference quality-reviewer (not superpowers:code-reviewer), do NOT reference code-simplifier:code-simplifier (simplify step is orchestrator-direct /simplify), reference bead-implementor for write-tests/implement(/diagnose), and reference bead-verifier for verify.
- A trivial test bead processed through run-queue completes successfully with the new dispatches firing in the expected steps.
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

## Dependencies

- ✓ agents-config-7bk.1 (Phase 1 — bead-verifier, quality-reviewer delivered)
- ✓ agents-config-1ck (closed: *-developer agents removed; dynamic-dove preload superseded)
- (peer, non-blocking) agents-config-7bk.7 — cleanup of the code-simplifier agent layer
acceptance_criteria: src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/bead-implementor.md exists with required skills and tools. implement-bead SKILL.md documents and implements worktree-label lifecycle. implement-feature.formula.toml and fix-bug.formula.toml reference quality-reviewer (not superpowers:code-reviewer), do NOT reference code-simplifier subagent (simplify is orchestrator-direct /simplify), reference bead-implementor for write-tests/implement(/diagnose), and reference bead-verifier for verify. Trivial test bead processed through run-queue completes with new dispatches firing. Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

title: Externalize long bead specs to docs/beads/ files; bead description stays a short summary referencing the doc
notes:
Context: while brainstorming lu3.4, the final spec grew to ~300 lines. Writing the full spec into the bead notes field via 'bd update --notes' requires shell-escape gymnastics and replaces the field wholesale (see bug guard in lu3.4 spec R6). Manually archived lu3.4 spec to docs/specs/2026-04-19-ralf-it-form-reevaluation-design.md to preserve it.

Proposal: adopt a pattern where long bead specs live at docs/beads/<bead-id>-<slug>.md (or docs/specs/<date>-<slug>.md), and the bead's description/notes is a short summary + reference to the doc file path. The bead remains self-descriptive; the full spec is versioned in git alongside code.

Benefits:
- shell-safe authoring (edit the file directly, no CLI string escaping)
- PR-reviewable spec changes via normal diff review
- grep-friendly
- no duplication between bd's notes field and docs/specs
- bead description stays scannable in 'bd show <id>'

Open questions (for start-bead/brainstorm-bead to resolve, not now):
- Threshold for 'long enough' to externalize (line count? number of sections?)
- Should brainstorm-bead.formula.toml's write-spec step be updated to write long specs to file + summary to bd notes?
- Should create-bead / start-bead skills know about this pattern?
- Directory choice: reuse docs/specs/ vs new docs/beads/ subdir?
- How does the bead reference the doc — relative path? URL to repo? Commit-pinned?
- Do we need a 'seal' mechanism to prevent bd notes from drifting out of sync with the external doc?

title: wait-for-pr-comments: poll-copilot-review.sh returns stale reviews on re-review cycles
description:
# Problem

After pushing fixes and re-requesting Copilot review, poll-copilot-review.sh (in the wait-for-pr-comments skill) short-circuits on attempt 1 and reports copilot_review_found — but returns the STALE prior review rather than the new one. The JSON it emits has a commit_id field pointing at the OLD commit, which the skill's Phase 3 triage then operates on.

# Impact

Auto-fix loop spins on the same comments it already addressed. Discovered on agents-config-uvt PR #12 during two re-review cycles; had to fall back to manual gh api polling with a submitted_at > last-commit-timestamp filter.

# Root cause

The script searches /pulls/N/reviews for ANY review authored by Copilot. It doesn't compare commit_id against the latest pushed commit, nor submitted_at against the re-request time.

# Fix direction

poll-copilot-review.sh should accept an optional --since-commit or --since-timestamp argument, and the Phase 4 re-review flow in wait-for-pr-comments/SKILL.md should pass the latest commit's timestamp so only newer reviews match.

# Discovered-from

agents-config-uvt

comments.author: Scott Hamilton
comments.text:
Second repro on scotthamilton77/claude-code-sidekick PR #183 (2026-05-02):

- 11:05Z: Copilot review #1 with 4 inline findings.
- After fixes pushed, copilot_work_started fired at 14:08:33Z.
- poll-copilot-review.sh --skip-request-check entered sub-phase C, immediately found review #1 (predates work_started), reported 'Copilot review found (attempt 1)' and exited 0.
- Skill emitted Variant 1 'All fixed, no Copilot re-review' final report.
- Actual second review landed at 14:14:38Z (6 minutes later) with 6 NEW inline findings — caught manually, not by the script.

Adds two UX dimensions worth fixing alongside the timestamp filter:

1. Risk multiplier: a false 'All clear' Variant 1 report is the exact moment a user authorizes merge ('merge it' / 'ship it'). The skill needs to express uncertainty when a re-review is in flight, not declare clear.

2. Pre-trigger hint: even before copilot_work_started fires, the existence of commits with author.date > last_review.submitted_at is a strong signal a re-review may be queued. The report should surface that timing relationship explicitly so users can wait if needed.

Originally filed as scotthamilton77/claude-code-sidekick:sidekick-aga3 (now deleted as duplicate of this bead).

notes:
# Documentation pass for public repo readiness

## Summary
Prepare the agents-config repo for public consumption. Scope: polish existing top-level docs, correct known drift, and fill gaps a first-time visitor expects (LICENSE, CONTRIBUTING, per-subfolder READMEs). No GitHub-specific scaffolding, no prose-level rewrites.

## Background / motivation
Repo is going public. Current state: root README.md is detailed but has drift from the rules/ reorganization; root AGENTS.md has duplicated session-completion blocks; tool-specific subfolders under src/user/ have no or near-empty READMEs; no LICENSE file exists despite README declaring MIT; no CONTRIBUTING.md. A stranger landing on the repo needs accurate install guidance and a clear picture of what each folder contains.

Primary reader: fork-and-install users, not contributors. Light contributor notes only.

## Requirements
- Add a root LICENSE file matching the MIT license already declared in README.md. Copyright holder: "Scott Hamilton". Year: 2026.
- Add a short CONTRIBUTING.md acknowledging the repo is a personal config and asking contributors to open an issue first.
- Add a README.md to each tool-specific subfolder: src/user/.agents/, src/user/.claude/, src/user/.codex/, src/user/.gemini/. Each explains purpose, contents, install target, and intended audience.
- Correct drift in existing root README.md and root AGENTS.md: any stale references to file paths, section names, or structure that have since moved (notably content relocated from CLAUDE-EXTENSIONS.md to rules/).
- Verify README.md Skills table and Agents section against the actual contents of src/user/.agents/skills/ and src/user/.agents/agents/.
- Delete the 4-line stub at src/user/.claude/AGENTS.md in the same PR that adds src/user/.claude/README.md (README added first, stub removed after).

## Out of scope
- .github/ scaffolding (issue templates, PR templates, workflows)
- CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
- Restructuring or rewriting the root README for style
- Changes to docs/plans/ or docs/specs/ content
- Changes to src/plugins/AGENTS.md (already in good shape) — but root README tree SHOULD mention src/plugins/
- Any non-doc changes (no code edits, no install.sh changes)
- Any edits inside the root AGENTS.md BEADS INTEGRATION v:1 marker block — auto-generated by the beads plugin, regenerated on bd runs. Only the non-generated Session Completion and Beads Quick Reference blocks that appear outside or alongside the markers should be deduplicated.

## Design notes

### Drift detection (README.md / AGENTS.md)

Concrete checks — after edits, these greps should return nothing (or only current-truth results):

- No instruction to "copy CLAUDE-EXTENSIONS.md.template" without also mentioning rules/.
- No claim that `<orchestration>`, `<delegation>`, `<completion-gate>`, `<delivery>`, or `<beads>` sections live in CLAUDE-EXTENSIONS.md. They live in INSTRUCTIONS.md (orchestration) and rules/ (the rest).
- No reference to a skill name that doesn't correspond to a directory in src/user/.agents/skills/.
- No reference to an agent name that doesn't correspond to a file in src/user/.agents/agents/.

### Skills/Agents verification

Bidirectional by directory name:
- Every directory in src/user/.agents/skills/ has a row in the README Skills table.
- Every row in the README Skills table corresponds to a real directory.
- Agents section either lists the shipping agent names or points to the directory; do NOT leave it as vague prose.

### Beads integration markers

Root AGENTS.md contains `<!-- BEGIN BEADS INTEGRATION v:1 ... -->` and `<!-- END BEADS INTEGRATION -->` markers around a plugin-managed block. This block is regenerated by the beads plugin on install and must not be edited by hand. Any deduplication work on AGENTS.md must target content OUTSIDE these markers.

### Subfolder README style

- Short (roughly 20-50 lines each). Short enough that a first-time visitor can scan.
- Link up to the root README for install flow; do not duplicate install instructions.
- Answer three questions per folder: what lives here, how does it get installed, who is it for.

### LICENSE

Canonical MIT license text. Format:
```
MIT License

Copyright (c) 2026 Scott Hamilton

Permission is hereby granted, free of charge, ...
```

### CONTRIBUTING.md

Example draft:

title: Empirical A/B testing of per-stage model/effort allocations against quality/cost outcomes
description:
Empirical A/B testing of the per-stage model/effort defaults (per 7bk.15) to validate cost vs quality outcomes with real bead processing. The F-04 default table shipped with 7bk.15 was set by argument, not data. This bead defines a methodology to gather data so future tuning is data-driven.

## Why

Several decisions in the F-04 table are calibration-by-intuition:
- preflight at sonnet-high (vs sonnet-medium): is the "architectural gate" framing right or paranoid?
- green-loop controller at opus-medium (vs opus-high): is medium effort enough for synthesis decisions?
- four haiku stages (quality-sweep, verify-ac, create-pr, merge-or-handoff): do they break down on edge cases, or stay mechanical?
- review-cycle at sonnet-medium: does it cope with high Copilot comment volume?
- diagnose (bug-class) at sonnet-high: is the cost justified by quality lift?
- red-tests Codex at gpt-5.5 vs gpt-5.4-mini: cost-vs-quality call (per TR5 reconsideration in 7bk.15)

Without empirical data, every adjustment to the table is argument-based; we may be silently sacrificing quality/reliability for cost or vice versa.

## Methodology sketch (refine at brainstorm)

1. Pick 5-10 representative beads from history (small/medium/large scope; feature/bug/chore mix; varying AC complexity).
2. For each bead, process through the pipeline N times with different stage-defaults variants:
   - Variant A: F-04 baseline (per 7bk.15 amendment 2026-05-04)
   - Variant B: cheaper (downgrade preflight + diagnose to sonnet-medium; opus-medium → sonnet-high in green-loop)
   - Variant C: expensive (upgrade four haiku stages to sonnet-medium)
3. Capture per-run metrics:
   - Total token cost (Anthropic + foreign-CLI)
   - Wall-time per stage and total
   - # of RALF iterations to converge
   - # of Copilot comments (FIX-class)
   - # of human escapes (flag-human invocations)
   - # of reverts/regressions in subsequent commits
4. Quality scoring: human (or independent reviewer agent) reviews each variant's PR, scores 1-5 on:
   - (a) test correctness
   - (b) implementation quality
   - (c) AC coverage
   - (d) idiomatic / non-hacky
5. Output: a calibration report with per-stage recommendations to amend the F-04 table.

## Dependencies

- agents-config-7bk.9 (per-stage architecture functional)
- agents-config-7bk.14 (Model:/Effort: directives mechanism shipped — required to vary defaults per run)
- agents-config-7bk.15 (initial defaults applied as baseline)

## Out of scope

- Changing the F-04 table without supporting data from this exercise.
- Building automated A/B harness in v1 (manual comparison runs are acceptable for first pass).
- Cross-project generalization (this is project-specific calibration).

## Acceptance Criteria

- A/B test methodology documented and approved
- Test fixture beads selected (5-10 representative)
- Comparison runs executed for at least 3 variants
- Quality scoring rubric agreed and applied
- Calibration report produced with specific recommendations to amend the F-04 table (or confirm it as-is)
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

title: Cost rightsizing: per-stage model/effort surgery across brainstorm and green-loop
description:
Authoritative source for the per-stage default model/effort table consumed by 7bk.14's directives mechanism. Plus six cost-rightsizing recommendations to apply once the per-stage architecture (7bk.9) is functional.

Full design: `docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md` §9 (Cost model).

## Per-stage default model/effort table

These defaults are owned by THIS bead. They are consumed by 7bk.14 (Model: and Effort: directives mechanism) and applied via formula step body directives at pour time.

| Stage              | Model               | Effort | Rationale                                                       |
|--------------------|---------------------|--------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|
| preflight          | claude-sonnet-4-6   | high   | Architectural gate; bad decisions corrupt whole pipeline; cheap absolute cost |
| red-tests          | claude-sonnet-4-6   | medium | TDD orchestration; moderate complexity                           |
| green-loop         | claude-opus-4-7     | medium | RALF-IT controller (already opus per skill frontmatter); synthesis is highest-leverage |
| quality-sweep      | claude-haiku-4-5    | medium | Mostly shell-out (lint/build/typecheck/test/static-analysis)     |
| verify-ac          | claude-haiku-4-5    | medium | Mechanical AC matching against worktree state                    |
| create-pr          | claude-haiku-4-5    | medium | gh pr create + structured PR body composition                    |
| review-cycle       | claude-sonnet-4-6   | medium | Per-comment classify + dispatch fix subagents + reply            |
| merge-or-handoff   | claude-haiku-4-5    | medium | Branch-and-stamp                                                 |

Bug-class addition (per architecture doc §3 diagnose stage):

| Stage              | Model               | Effort | Rationale                                                       |
|--------------------|---------------------|--------|-----------------------------------------------------------------|
| diagnose (bug-only) | claude-sonnet-4-6  | high   | Root-cause investigation; novel reasoning over unknown bug surface |

Subagent dispatches inside each stage have their own defaults per agent frontmatter (e.g., bead-implementor, bead-verifier, quality-reviewer).

These defaults are calibration-by-argument, not data. Empirical validation is tracked in 7bk.16.

## Tier 3 cost-rightsizing recommendations

### TR1 — bead-spec persona split (overlaps with 7bk.3)
- assess + classify-and-policy → claude-sonnet-4-6 effort:medium
- write-spec → claude-opus-4-7 effort:high
- ralf-spec-review → claude-opus-4-7 effort:medium with FRESH context (per 7bk.3's amended decomposition)

### TR2 — Drop simplify from per-iteration RALF; final pass only
RALF-IT skill: quality-reviewer runs per iteration; simplify slash command runs ONCE at final review pass.

### TR3 — bead-implementor effort:high for green-loop iter 1 only
First RALF iteration: bead-implementor runs effort:high (anchors architectural choices). Subsequent iterations: effort:medium. Knob: `iteration-effort-green-loop-1: high` (or configure as RALF-IT default).

### TR4 — quality-reviewer drop opus[1m] → opus 4.7 for per-iteration
Per-iteration reviews: claude-opus-4-7 (standard 200K context). Final review pass on full bead diff: claude-opus-4-7[1m]. Implementation via context-mode knob on the agent dispatch.

### TR5 — red-tests Codex gpt-5.5 cost-vs-quality call (DEFERRED)
Stage 2 keeps gpt-5.5 default (canonical post-2026-05-04 release). Whether to downgrade to gpt-5.4-mini for test review is a cost-vs-quality decision deferred to empirical calibration via 7bk.16. Revisit if data supports downgrade.

### TR6 — quality-reviewer skill preload hygiene
Remove preloaded skills `superpowers:requesting-code-review` and `superpowers:receiving-code-review` from quality-reviewer's frontmatter (they are for the requestor and receiver of reviews, not the reviewer itself). Either no preloads or `superpowers:verification-before-completion`.

## Aggregate impact estimate

Per-bead worst-case: ~$13-17 → ~$8-10 (35-45% reduction).
- Brainstorm: ~$3 → ~$1.50 (TR1)
- green-loop: ~$8-12 → ~$5-7 (TR2 + TR3 + TR4)
- red-tests: ~$1 → ~$0.50 (TR5 if applied per 7bk.16)
- Hygiene: trivial savings (TR6)

## Dependencies

- 7bk.14 (formula model/effort directives — the mechanism)
- 7bk.3 (TR1's bead-spec split lands there)
- 7bk.9 (per-stage architecture is the surface being tuned)

## Acceptance Criteria

- TR1-TR6 applied per spec (TR5 conditionally per 7bk.16 data)
- Per-stage default table reflected in `implement-feature.formula.toml` + `fix-bug.formula.toml` via Model: and Effort: directives
- Smoke test: process bead end-to-end; verify each stage uses expected model/effort per the new defaults
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

title: Phase 3: bead-spec agent + brainstorm-bead formula rewiring
description:
Replace single-agent brainstorm support with role-specialized bead-spec personas, drop user-scoped memory, and restore the fresh-eyes property for spec review.

Full design: `docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md` §4.4 (Decomposed bead-spec personas) and §6 (Agent catalog).

## Why decomposed

A single bead-spec agent driving both write-spec and ralf-spec-review is the classic self-review anti-pattern: the spec writer reviews its own prior work with its own biases pre-loaded. `memory: user` further accumulates context across all brainstorm sessions for all projects, contaminating assessments with irrelevant prior project context. RALF-IT's own doctrine forbids this pattern.

Folded scope from agents-config-8sy (closes when this bead lands).

## Changes

1. Create multiple bead-spec personas (names TBD at implementation; suggested: bead-assessor, bead-specwriter, bead-reviewer):
   - All `claude-opus-4-7`
   - Effort: per-role calibration per architecture doc §6 and 7bk.15
   - `memory: NONE` (no user-scoped memory)
   - Skills: per-role (assessor: brainstorming; specwriter: brainstorming + writing-plans; reviewer: brainstorming only)
   - Tools: read-only (Read+Grep+Glob)
   - Color: distinct per persona

2. Update `brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` — dispatch the appropriate agent per step:
   - assess → bead-assessor
   - write-spec → bead-specwriter
   - classify-and-policy (from 7bk.12) → bead-assessor or bead-specwriter (decide at implementation)
   - ralf-spec-review → bead-reviewer (DIFFERENT persona from specwriter — fresh-eyes property restored)

3. End-to-end test: brainstorm session dispatches each role correctly with no shared memory contamination.

## On close of this bead

Close agents-config-8sy as folded.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Multiple bead-spec personas exist at `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/`
- `brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` dispatches the appropriate persona per step
- ralf-spec-review uses a DIFFERENT persona than write-spec
- No persona uses `memory: user`
- E2E brainstorm session works correctly with each role active
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

title: Audit and prune unused skills, agents, and commands
description:
The agents-config repo has accumulated a large inventory of skills, agents, and slash commands across src/user/.agents/, src/user/.claude/, and src/plugins/. Some of these are actively used, others have never been invoked in practice. This audit-and-prune task identifies which are carrying their weight and which are dead weight.

## Why this exists
Over time, skills/agents/commands get added speculatively or for one-off needs and then never revisited. Every extra entry in the inventory is cognitive overhead for the agent at selection time and maintenance burden for the user. Pruning unused entries keeps the configuration sharp and the agent's attention focused on the tools that actually pay rent.

## What needs to be done
- Inventory all skills (src/user/.agents/skills/, src/plugins/*/skills/), agents (src/user/.agents/agents/), and commands (src/user/.claude/commands/, src/plugins/*/commands/) currently installed
- Gather usage evidence: grep transcripts/session logs if available, ask the user which ones they recognize using, cross-reference against recent PRs and commits for skills invoked
- Categorize each entry:
  - KEEP — actively used, clearly valuable
  - PRUNE — never used, redundant, or superseded
  - UNCERTAIN — escalate to user for judgment
- For PRUNE candidates: confirm with user, then remove from the repo
- For UNCERTAIN: present a short summary (name + description + last-seen-used signal) and let the user decide
- Update any references (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, skill indexes) after removals

## Notes
- This is a capture bead — brainstorm/spec work happens later when it's picked up
- Scope excludes the plugin-provided inventories (beads plugin, superpowers plugin, codex plugin, etc.) — those are managed upstream
- Consider whether a recurring lightweight audit (quarterly?) would be useful as a follow-on

title: Discovered-work snippets use <placeholder> inside bash if-condition — invalid shell if copy/pasted
design: Small-scope follow-up: three near-identical doc edits. Fits a single worktree + PR. Same methodology as 9cz (worktree → fix → code-reviewer + simplifier → PR → Copilot → merge-and-cleanup).
notes:
# Problem

In PR #13 (now merged as db41812), Copilot's second review round flagged 3 inline snippets where the discovered-work decision uses:

    if [ -n "$PARENT" ] && <work-is-sibling-subtask-of-$PARENT>; then
      bd create "..." -t <type> -p <prio> --parent "$PARENT"
    else
      NEW=$(bd create "..." -t <type> -p <prio> --json | jq -r '.id')
      bd dep add "$NEW" {{bead-id}} --type discovered-from
    fi

The `<work-is-sibling-subtask-of-$PARENT>` token is a placeholder, but in bash boolean context `<` is parsed as input redirect — so literal copy-paste would try to open a file. Unlike `<type>`/`<prio>` (placeholders inside string args, safe), the if-condition placeholder is syntactically dangerous.

Copilot's suggestion: introduce an `IS_SIBLING_SUBTASK=true/false` variable (or split the if into two separate command sequences), so the snippet is runnable-looking even if placeholder values haven't been substituted.

# Affected files

- src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/create-bead/SKILL.md line 77
- src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml line 319 (housekeeping step 4)
- src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml line 314 (housekeeping step 4)

# Copilot comment IDs

- 3107495666 (create-bead)
- 3107495677 (implement-feature)
- 3107495680 (fix-bug)

# Acceptance

- All three if-condition snippets replaced with shell-valid form (commented `IS_SIBLING_SUBTASK` boolean, or split into two explicit command paths).
- No more `<something-with-bash-chars>` placeholders inside boolean/expression contexts.
- Reply to each of the 3 Copilot comments pointing at the fix commit; resolve the 3 threads on the new PR.
- TOML still parses.

# Discovered-from

Discovered during wait-for-pr-comments round 2 on PR #13 (agents-config-9cz). Originating bead has no parent epic, so this is orphan + discovered-from (not an epic sibling).

title: wait-for-pr-comments: re-requesting Copilot via add-reviewer is idempotent
description:
# Problem

gh pr edit <N> --add-reviewer @copilot is idempotent when Copilot is already on the reviewer list — no copilot_work_started event fires, and no fresh review is produced. wait-for-pr-comments Phase 4's re-review path currently relies on the user (or the agent) running add-reviewer to trigger round 2+, which silently fails after round 1.

# Impact

Observed on agents-config-uvt PR #12 round 3: pushed 77bfa98, called gh pr edit --add-reviewer @copilot, waited 5+ min, no copilot_work_started fired. Had to emit a final report without round-3 feedback.

# Fix direction

Add a sub-step in Phase 4: before (or instead of) add-reviewer, run gh pr edit <N> --remove-reviewer @copilot, then --add-reviewer @copilot. The remove+add pair reliably triggers a fresh review cycle. Alternatively, document the gotcha in the skill's Phase 4 so operators know to force-refresh manually.

title: Phase 1.5: evaluate risk-closers before fork escalation
description:
# Context

After `agents-config-lu3.1` (wisp materialization fix) and `agents-config-lu3.2` (beads ↔ superpowers partnership contract) ship, residual risks remain that could still cause agents to drift from the bead protocol. Analysis in session `c06c7b55` estimated the combined fixes reduce Divergence-1/2-style failures by ~60–75% in a fresh session, but leave gaps — especially around user-directed drift and skill-description magnetism.

This bead exists to **brainstorm, decide on, and (if chosen) implement a set of non-fork alternatives before escalating to `agents-config-cx6` (fork superpowers → beadpowers plugin).**

# Purpose

Decide whether any of the four risk-closers below are worth taking, and in what order, BEFORE paying the maintenance cost of a superpowers fork.

Only after this bead is resolved (options decided + implemented OR explicitly deferred) does the fork bead `agents-config-cx6` become unblocked.

# Candidate options to evaluate

## Option A: Hook-level enforcement

A PreToolUse hook that blocks the `Skill` tool from invoking `superpowers:writing-plans`, `superpowers:executing-plans`, or `superpowers:subagent-driven-development` when a bead is in progress (claimed or in_progress state detectable via bd).

- **Pro**: active enforcement, not passive rule compliance
- **Con**: requires hook development + maintenance; hook may be noisy if bead state detection is imperfect
- **Effort**: medium

## Option B: User-direction guardrail

When the user directly requests ralf-it, writing-plans, executing-plans, or subagent-driven-development on a bead, the agent surfaces `implement-bead` as the bead-workflow alternative and asks the user to confirm. Lives as a red flag in `start-bead` / `implement-bead` SKILL.md or as a rule in `beads.md`.

- **Pro**: very cheap (~3 lines); closes the single highest-impact residual risk (user-directed drift)
- **Con**: adds friction to every user-directed skill invocation on a bead; user may find it annoying
- **Effort**: low
- **Note**: this was proposed as "Fix F" in the Divergence 2 brainstorm and explicitly deferred pending this evaluation

## Option C: Session-audit cadence

Schedule a recurring audit (e.g. weekly, or every N sessions) that scans transcripts for partnership-contract violations: instances where an agent invoked writing-plans / executing-plans / subagent-driven-development while a bead was claimed. Use a scheduled cron trigger (see `superpowers:schedule` skill) or manual cadence.

- **Pro**: detects drift early; low-cost monitoring
- **Con**: detection ≠ prevention; process overhead
- **Effort**: low-medium (depends on how automated)

## Option D: Broaden the failure-mode block in start-bead

Current Divergence 1 fix covers only "0/0 steps after wisp creation" as the named failure mode. Broaden to cover:
- "X/Y already done" (re-entry into a completed molecule)
- "blocked with no [ready] steps" (dependency misconfiguration)
- CLI error states from bd commands
- Interactive step re-entry (how does agent know to advance after user answers in the discuss step?)

Each state gets a diagnostic + recovery action in `start-bead` SKILL.md.

- **Pro**: closes several narrow-but-real edge cases from the Divergence 1 analysis
- **Con**: more skill text to maintain; risk of over-engineering
- **Effort**: low

# Decision process

1. **Observe lu3.2 in the wild**: need at least 2 fresh session audits after the partnership contract ships to establish a baseline drift rate
2. **Brainstorm session**: weigh each option against observed drift patterns
3. **Record decisions**: use `bd decision` for each option (accepted / deferred / rejected with rationale)
4. **Implement accepted options**: each becomes a sub-bead, dependency on this one
5. **Resolve this bead**: close with outcome summary
6. **Unblock cx6 or close cx6**: either escalate to fork, or close cx6 as "no longer needed"

# Trigger conditions to start this bead

Do NOT start this bead until:
- `agents-config-lu3.1` is merged AND
- `agents-config-lu3.2` is merged AND
- At least 2 subsequent session audits have been performed to gather drift baseline data

# Acceptance criteria

- Each of the four options has a `bd decision` record: accepted / deferred / rejected with rationale
- Any accepted options are implemented (as sub-beads) OR explicitly scheduled
- Final outcome summary written to this bead
- `agents-config-cx6` (fork bead) is either unblocked (go fork) or closed (partnership + risk-closers are sufficient)
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass. (for any implemented options)

# Notes

- This bead BLOCKS `agents-config-cx6`. The fork cannot proceed until this evaluation is complete.
- Residual risks list compiled from session `c06c7b55` analysis of Divergence 1 + 2 fix confidence
- Option B (user-direction guardrail) was explicitly deferred from lu3.2 scope for evaluation here
- If all four options are rejected AND lu3.2 alone is sufficient in practice, close cx6 — no fork needed

title: Phase 2: fork superpowers → curated bead-aware plugin (escalation from lu3.2)
description:
# Context

Phase 2 escalation from `agents-config-lu3.2` (beads ↔ superpowers partnership contract).

The partnership contract approach (overlay rules) relies on agents respecting "off-limits" guidance for the three competing superpowers skills: `writing-plans`, `executing-plans`, and `subagent-driven-development`. If audits of future sessions continue to show agents pulled by those skills' triggers despite the rules, the structural fix is to remove them from the agent's skill inventory entirely.

This bead captures that escalation path. **Do NOT start this work unless overlay rules prove insufficient** — the partnership contract is much lower maintenance and should be given time to prove itself first.

# Scope (when the time comes)

- Fork or selectively borrow the "keeper" superpowers skills into a new plugin
- Drop the three competing skills entirely (they won't exist in the plugin's skill registry at all)
- Where useful, rewrite keeper skills to be bead-aware (cite bead IDs, labels, formula terminology)
- Users installing the new plugin get the curated set; superpowers itself can remain installed for non-bead projects

## Candidate keepers (to import)

- `test-driven-development`
- `verification-before-completion`
- `systematic-debugging`
- `root-cause-tracing`
- `using-git-worktrees`
- `finishing-a-development-branch`
- `requesting-code-review`
- `receiving-code-review`
- `brainstorming`
- `dispatching-parallel-agents`
- `writing-skills` (if we want bead-aware skill authoring)

## Explicitly dropped

- `writing-plans`
- `executing-plans`
- `subagent-driven-development`

## Plugin naming (decide at implementation time)

Candidate names — architect's call:
- `superbeadpowers`
- `superbeads`
- `beadpowers`

# Trigger conditions

Do NOT start this work unless at least one of:
- 2+ subsequent session audits show agents drifting to off-limits skills despite `agents-config-lu3.2` overlay rules being in place
- A user or operator reports the partnership contract is not holding
- Structural need arises (e.g. a keeper skill needs bead-awareness that can't be layered via rules)

# Trade-offs to weigh at implementation time

- **Maintenance cost**: forked skills need manual upstream sync; estimated hours/month
- **Upstream divergence**: if superpowers evolves the keepers, we miss improvements unless we re-sync
- **Plugin packaging**: decide marketplace vs. vendored vs. symlinked install strategy
- **Scope creep risk**: tempting to rewrite everything once forking — resist, keep edits minimal

# Design decisions (deferred)

All implementation details deferred to brainstorming when trigger conditions are met. Capture here only: the decision to fork selectively rather than overlay-only, and the list of keepers vs. drops.

# Acceptance criteria (placeholder — refine at brainstorm time)

- New plugin created under `src/plugins/<name>/` with keepers imported
- Three competing skills confirmed ABSENT from the plugin
- Bead plugin rules updated to prefer the new plugin's versions
- Install path documented
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

# Notes

- Blocked-by: `agents-config-lu3.2` must be merged and observed in practice first
- Do NOT start without user approval — capturing here only to preserve the escalation option
- Ref: strategy discussion in session `c06c7b55`

title: Beads plugin: suppress 'git add failed' auto-export warning when .beads/issues.jsonl is gitignored
description: After merging PR #9 in agents-config (which gitignored .beads/issues.jsonl for PII reasons), every bd command now prints a harmless 'Warning: auto-export: git add failed: exit status 1' because the beads post-command hook tries to git-add a file that is now ignored by .gitignore. The Dolt push path works correctly — this is purely a warning hygiene issue. Fix: the beads plugin's auto-export step should check 'git check-ignore' (or equivalent) against the export path before running 'git add', and silently skip the add when the path is ignored. Affects any project that chooses the Dolt-only sync pattern.

title: Beads plugin duplicates Session Completion block in AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md
description: The beads plugin's BEADS INTEGRATION marker block renders the same 'Session Completion' and 'Beads Issue Tracker' content into both root AGENTS.md and root CLAUDE.md. This is plugin-managed (inside BEGIN/END BEADS INTEGRATION markers) so manual deduplication is impossible. Options: (a) file upstream issue against the beads plugin to support a 'pointer-only' profile for the secondary file, or (b) add a non-plugin pointer pattern in our templates and stop using the minimal profile for CLAUDE.md. Discovered during agents-config-mxe public-repo docs pass.

title: Tighten brainstorm-bead.formula.toml finalize Report-block voice
description:
# Context

The brainstorm-bead formula's finalize step contains a 'Report to the user' block that speaks in awkwardly mixed voice: nominally what the agent reports to the user, but includes imperatives like 'Do NOT continue to implementation in this session unless you explicitly authorize it' — reading as the agent giving the USER an imperative about the AGENT's own behavior.

# Previous action

The first code-simplifier pass on agents-config-uvt PR #12 flagged this as a candidate for a future tightening pass but declined to reshape it under the 'smallest change that improves clarity' bar.

# Proposed direction

Rewrite to first-person-agent voice:
  'I will stop here. The default is: another agent (run-queue, in a dedicated session) picks up implementation. To implement in this session instead, say explicitly ("implement it now", "do <bead-id> next", etc.) — otherwise I will not continue.'
